   171→            Expect.isTrue (markers.Contains("conditional,")) "Middleware should execute when condition is true"
   172→        }
   173→
   174→        testTask "plugBeforeRoutingWhen skips middleware when condition is false" {
   175→            let alwaysFalse (_: IApplicationBuilder) = false
   176→            let builder = WebHostBuilder([||])
   177→            let spec =
   178→                WebHostSpec.Empty
   179→                |> fun s -> builder.PlugBeforeRoutingWhen(s, alwaysFalse, fun app ->
   180→                    app.Use(Func<HttpContext, RequestDelegate, Task>(fun ctx next ->
   181→                        markerMiddleware "conditional" next ctx)))
   182→
   183→            let client = createTestServer (fun _ -> spec)
   184→            let! (response : HttpResponseMessage) = client.GetAsync("/test")
   185→            let markers =

   187→                    response.Headers.GetValues("X-Markers") |> Seq.head

   190→
   191→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Should return 200 OK"
   192→            Expect.isFalse (markers.Contains("conditional,")) "Middleware should NOT execute when condition is false"
   193→        }
   194→
   195→        // Tests for plugBeforeRoutingWhenNot (User Story 2)
   196→        testTask "plugBeforeRoutingWhenNot executes middleware when condition is false" {
   197→            let alwaysFalse (_: IApplicationBuilder) = false
   198→            let builder = WebHostBuilder([||])
   199→            let spec =

   201→                |> fun s -> builder.PlugBeforeRoutingWhenNot(s, alwaysFalse, fun app ->
   202→                    app.Use(Func<HttpContext, RequestDelegate, Task>(fun ctx next ->
   203→                        markerMiddleware "conditional" next ctx)))
   204→
   205→            let client = createTestServer (fun _ -> spec)
   206→            let! (response : HttpResponseMessage) = client.GetAsync("/test")
   207→            let markers =

   213→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Should return 200 OK"
   214→            Expect.isTrue (markers.Contains("conditional,")) "Middleware should execute when condition is false (WhenNot)"
   215→        }
   216→
   217→        testTask "plugBeforeRoutingWhenNot skips middleware when condition is true" {
   218→            let alwaysTrue (_: IApplicationBuilder) = true
   219→            let builder = WebHostBuilder([||])
   220→            let spec =
   221→                WebHostSpec.Empty
   222→                |> fun s -> builder.PlugBeforeRoutingWhenNot(s, alwaysTrue, fun app ->
   223→                    app.Use(Func<HttpContext, RequestDelegate, Task>(fun ctx next ->
   224→                        markerMiddleware "conditional" next ctx)))
   225→
   226→            let client = createTestServer (fun _ -> spec)
   227→            let! (response : HttpResponseMessage) = client.GetAsync("/test")
   228→            let markers =
   229→                if response.Headers.Contains("X-Markers") then

   234→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Should return 200 OK"
   235→            Expect.isFalse (markers.Contains("conditional,")) "Middleware should NOT execute when condition is true (WhenNot)"
   236→        }
   237→
   238→        // Tests for composition (User Story 3)
   239→        testTask "Multiple conditional before-routing middleware compose correctly" {
   240→            let alwaysTrue (_: IApplicationBuilder) = true
   241→            let alwaysFalse (_: IApplicationBuilder) = false
   242→            let builder = WebHostBuilder([||])
   243→            let spec =
   244→                WebHostSpec.Empty
   245→                |> fun s -> builder.PlugBeforeRoutingWhen(s, alwaysTrue, fun app ->
   246→                    app.Use(Func<HttpContext, RequestDelegate, Task>(fun ctx next ->
   247→                        markerMiddleware "first" next ctx)))
   248→                |> fun s -> builder.PlugBeforeRoutingWhen(s, alwaysFalse, fun app ->
   249→                    app.Use(Func<HttpContext, RequestDelegate, Task>(fun ctx next ->
   250→                        markerMiddleware "skipped" next ctx)))

   252→                    app.Use(Func<HttpContext, RequestDelegate, Task>(fun ctx next ->
   253→                        markerMiddleware "second" next ctx)))
   254→
   255→            let client = createTestServer (fun _ -> spec)

   257→            let markers =

   263→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Should return 200 OK"
   264→            Expect.isTrue (markers.Contains("first,")) "First middleware should execute"
   265→            Expect.isFalse (markers.Contains("skipped,")) "Skipped middleware should NOT execute"
   266→            Expect.isTrue (markers.Contains("second,")) "Second middleware should execute"
   267→        }
   268→
   269→        testTask "Conditional before-routing middleware works with regular plugBeforeRouting" {

   281→            let client = createTestServer (fun _ -> spec)

   290→            Expect.isTrue (markers.Contains("unconditional,")) "Unconditional middleware should execute"

1→namespace Frank.Cli.Core.Extraction
     2→
     3→open System
     4→open VDS.RDF
     5→open Frank.Cli.Core.Rdf
     6→open Frank.Cli.Core.Rdf.Vocabularies
     7→open Frank.Cli.Core.Analysis
     8→
     9→/// Maps HTTP methods to Schema.org Actions and Hydra operations.
    10→module CapabilityMapper =
    11→
    12→    let private httpMethodToSchemaAction (method: HttpMethod) : string =
    13→        match method with
    14→        | Get -> SchemaOrg.ReadAction
    15→        | Post -> SchemaOrg.CreateAction
    16→        | Put -> SchemaOrg.UpdateAction
    17→        | Delete -> SchemaOrg.DeleteAction
    18→        | Patch -> SchemaOrg.UpdateAction
    19→        | Head -> SchemaOrg.ReadAction
    20→        | Options -> SchemaOrg.ReadAction
    21→
    22→    let private httpMethodToString (method: HttpMethod) : string =
    23→        match method with
    24→        | Get -> "GET"
    25→        | Post -> "POST"
    26→        | Put -> "PUT"
    27→        | Delete -> "DELETE"
    28→        | Patch -> "PATCH"
    29→        | Head -> "HEAD"
    30→        | Options -> "OPTIONS"
    31→
    32→    let private routeToSlug (route: string) =
    33→        route.TrimStart('/').Replace("/", "_").Replace("{", "").Replace("}", "")
    34→
    35→    let private resourceUri (config: TypeMapper.MappingConfig) (route: string) =
    36→        let base' = config.BaseUri.ToString().TrimEnd('/')
    37→        let cleanRoute = route.TrimStart('/')
    38→        Uri(base' + "/resources/" + cleanRoute)
    39→
    40→    let mapCapabilities (config: TypeMapper.MappingConfig) (resources: AnalyzedResource list) : IGraph =
    41→        let graph = createGraph ()
    42→        let rdfType = createUriNode graph (Uri Rdf.Type)
    43→
    44→        for res in resources do
    45→            let resUri = resourceUri config res.RouteTemplate
    46→            let resNode = createUriNode graph resUri
    47→            let slug = routeToSlug res.RouteTemplate
    48→
    49→            for method in res.HttpMethods do
    50→                let methodStr = httpMethodToString method
    51→                let blankId = sprintf "op_%s_%s" slug (methodStr.ToLowerInvariant())
    52→                let opNode = createBlankNode graph blankId
    53→
    54→                // rdf:type hydra:Operation
    55→                assertTriple graph (opNode, rdfType, createUriNode graph (Uri Hydra.Operation))
    56→
    57→                // rdf:type schema:{ActionType}
    58→                let actionUri = httpMethodToSchemaAction method
    59→                assertTriple graph (opNode, rdfType, createUriNode graph (Uri actionUri))
    60→
    61→                // hydra:method
    62→                assertTriple graph (
    63→                    opNode,
    64→                    createUriNode graph (Uri Hydra.Method),
    65→                    createLiteralNode graph methodStr (Some(Uri Xsd.String)))
    66→
    67→                // link resource to operation
    68→                assertTriple graph (resNode, createUriNode graph (Uri Hydra.SupportedOperation), opNode)
    69→
    70→        graph
    71→

1→module Frank.Auth.Tests.AuthorizationTests
     2→
     3→open System
     4→open System.Net
     5→open System.Net.Http
     6→open System.Security.Claims
     7→open System.Threading.Tasks
     8→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication
     9→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authorization
    10→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder
    11→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting
    12→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http
    13→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing
    14→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.TestHost
    15→open Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection
    16→open Microsoft.Extensions.FileProviders
    17→open Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting
    18→open Expecto
    19→open Frank.Builder
    20→open Frank.Auth
    21→
    22→/// Test authentication scheme name
    23→let [<Literal>] TestScheme = "TestScheme"
    24→
    25→/// Simple endpoint data source for tests
    26→type TestEndpointDataSource(endpoints: Endpoint[]) =
    27→    inherit EndpointDataSource()
    28→    override _.Endpoints = endpoints :> _
    29→    override _.GetChangeToken() = NullChangeToken.Singleton :> _
    30→
    31→/// Test authentication handler that authenticates based on X-Test-User header.
    32→type TestAuthHandler(options, logger, encoder) =
    33→    inherit AuthenticationHandler<AuthenticationSchemeOptions>(options, logger, encoder)
    34→
    35→    override this.HandleAuthenticateAsync() =
    36→        let headerValue = this.Request.Headers["X-Test-User"].ToString()
    37→        if String.IsNullOrEmpty headerValue then
    38→            Task.FromResult(AuthenticateResult.NoResult())
    39→        else
    40→            let claims = ResizeArray<Claim>()
    41→            claims.Add(Claim(ClaimTypes.Name, headerValue))
    42→
    43→            let claimsHeader = this.Request.Headers["X-Test-Claims"].ToString()
    44→            if not (String.IsNullOrEmpty claimsHeader) then
    45→                for part in claimsHeader.Split(';') do
    46→                    let kv = part.Split('=', 2)
    47→                    if kv.Length = 2 then
    48→                        claims.Add(Claim(kv[0], kv[1]))
    49→
    50→            let rolesHeader = this.Request.Headers["X-Test-Roles"].ToString()
    51→            if not (String.IsNullOrEmpty rolesHeader) then
    52→                for role in rolesHeader.Split(';') do
    53→                    claims.Add(Claim(ClaimTypes.Role, role))
    54→
    55→            let identity = ClaimsIdentity(claims, TestScheme)
    56→            let principal = ClaimsPrincipal(identity)
    57→            let ticket = AuthenticationTicket(principal, TestScheme)
    58→            Task.FromResult(AuthenticateResult.Success(ticket))
    59→
    60→/// Creates a test server with Frank resource definitions.
    61→let createAuthTestServer (resources: Resource list) (configureServices: IServiceCollection -> unit) =
    62→    let allEndpoints = resources |> List.collect (fun r -> r.Endpoints |> Array.toList) |> List.toArray
    63→    let builder =
    64→        Host.CreateDefaultBuilder([||])
    65→            .ConfigureWebHost(fun webBuilder ->
    66→                webBuilder
    67→                    .UseTestServer()
    68→                    .ConfigureServices(fun services ->
    69→                        services.AddRouting() |> ignore
    70→                        services.AddAuthentication(TestScheme)
    71→                            .AddScheme<AuthenticationSchemeOptions, TestAuthHandler>(TestScheme, fun _ -> ())
    72→                            |> ignore
    73→                        services.AddAuthorization() |> ignore
    74→                        configureServices services)
    75→                    .Configure(fun app ->
    76→                        app
    77→                            .UseRouting()
    78→                            .UseAuthentication()
    79→                            .UseAuthorization()
    80→                            .UseEndpoints(fun endpoints ->
    81→                                endpoints.DataSources.Add(TestEndpointDataSource(allEndpoints)))
    82→                        |> ignore)
    83→                |> ignore)
    84→
    85→    let host = builder.Build()
    86→    host.Start()
    87→    host.GetTestClient()
    88→
    89→/// Helper to send a request with optional test user and claims
    90→let sendRequest (client: HttpClient) (method: HttpMethod) (path: string) (user: string option) (claims: string option) (roles: string option) =
    91→    task {
    92→        let request = new HttpRequestMessage(method, path)
    93→        user |> Option.iter (fun u -> request.Headers.Add("X-Test-User", u))
    94→        claims |> Option.iter (fun c -> request.Headers.Add("X-Test-Claims", c))
    95→        roles |> Option.iter (fun r -> request.Headers.Add("X-Test-Roles", r))
    96→        return! client.SendAsync(request)
    97→    }
    98→
    99→let simpleHandler : RequestDelegate =
   100→    RequestDelegate(fun ctx -> ctx.Response.WriteAsync("OK"))
   101→
   102→// ===== US5: Application-Level Auth Configuration =====
   103→
   104→[<Tests>]
   105→let us5Tests =
   106→    testList "US5 - Application-Level Auth Configuration" [
   107→        testTask "useAuthentication + useAuthorization wiring enables the authorization pipeline" {
   108→            let protectedResource =
   109→                resource "/protected" {
   110→                    name "Protected"
   111→                    requireAuth
   112→                    get simpleHandler
   113→                }
   114→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ protectedResource ] ignore
   115→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/protected" None None None
   116→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.Unauthorized "Unauthenticated request to protected resource should return 401"
   117→        }
   118→
   119→        testTask "requireAuth returns 200 for authenticated requests when auth services are configured" {
   120→            let protectedResource =
   121→                resource "/protected" {
   122→                    name "Protected"
   123→                    requireAuth
   124→                    get simpleHandler
   125→                }
   126→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ protectedResource ] ignore
   127→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/protected" (Some "testuser") None None
   128→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Authenticated request to protected resource should return 200"
   129→        }
   130→    ]
   131→
   132→// ===== US1: Restrict Resource to Authenticated Users =====
   133→
   134→[<Tests>]
   135→let us1Tests =
   136→    testList "US1 - Restrict Resource to Authenticated Users" [
   137→        testTask "requireAuth + unauthenticated -> 401" {
   138→            let r = resource "/secure" { name "Secure"; requireAuth; get simpleHandler }
   139→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ r ] ignore
   140→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/secure" None None None
   141→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.Unauthorized "Should return 401"
   142→        }
   143→
   144→        testTask "requireAuth + authenticated -> 200 with handler executed" {
   145→            let handler = RequestDelegate(fun ctx -> ctx.Response.WriteAsync("handler-executed"))
   146→            let r = resource "/secure" { name "Secure"; requireAuth; get handler }
   147→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ r ] ignore
   148→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/secure" (Some "alice") None None
   149→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Should return 200"
   150→            let! body = response.Content.ReadAsStringAsync()
   151→            Expect.equal body "handler-executed" "Handler should have executed"
   152→        }
   153→
   154→        testTask "no auth operations -> publicly accessible regardless of auth status" {
   155→            let r = resource "/public" { name "Public"; get simpleHandler }
   156→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ r ] ignore
   157→
   158→            let! (response1: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/public" None None None
   159→            Expect.equal response1.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Unauthenticated should access public resource"
   160→
   161→            let! (response2: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/public" (Some "alice") None None
   162→            Expect.equal response2.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Authenticated should access public resource"
   163→        }
   164→    ]
   165→
   166→// ===== US2: Restrict Resource by Claim =====
   167→
   168→[<Tests>]
   169→let us2Tests =
   170→    testList "US2 - Restrict Resource by Claim" [
   171→        testTask "single-value claim match -> 200" {
   172→            let r = resource "/admin" { name "Admin"; requireClaim "scope" "admin"; get simpleHandler }
   173→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ r ] ignore
   174→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/admin" (Some "alice") (Some "scope=admin") None
   175→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "User with matching claim should get 200"
   176→        }
   177→
   178→        testTask "single-value claim mismatch -> 403" {
   179→            let r = resource "/admin" { name "Admin"; requireClaim "scope" "admin"; get simpleHandler }
   180→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ r ] ignore
   181→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/admin" (Some "alice") (Some "scope=read") None
   182→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.Forbidden "User without matching claim should get 403"
   183→        }
   184→
   185→        testTask "multi-value claim with any match -> 200" {
   186→            let r = resource "/data" { name "Data"; requireClaim "scope" ["read"; "write"]; get simpleHandler }
   187→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ r ] ignore
   188→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/data" (Some "alice") (Some "scope=read") None
   189→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "User with any matching claim value should get 200"
   190→        }
   191→
   192→        testTask "multi-value claim with no match -> 403" {
   193→            let r = resource "/data" { name "Data"; requireClaim "scope" ["read"; "write"]; get simpleHandler }
   194→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ r ] ignore
   195→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/data" (Some "alice") (Some "scope=delete") None
   196→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.Forbidden "User without any matching claim value should get 403"
   197→        }
   198→
   199→        testTask "two separate claim requirements with only one satisfied -> 403" {
   200→            let r = resource "/restricted" {
   201→                name "Restricted"
   202→                requireClaim "scope" "admin"
   203→                requireClaim "department" "engineering"
   204→                get simpleHandler
   205→            }
   206→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ r ] ignore
   207→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/restricted" (Some "alice") (Some "scope=admin") None
   208→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.Forbidden "User satisfying only one of two requirements should get 403"
   209→        }
   210→
   211→        testTask "two separate claim requirements both satisfied -> 200" {
   212→            let r = resource "/restricted" {
   213→                name "Restricted"
   214→                requireClaim "scope" "admin"
   215→                requireClaim "department" "engineering"
   216→                get simpleHandler
   217→            }
   218→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ r ] ignore
   219→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/restricted" (Some "alice") (Some "scope=admin;department=engineering") None
   220→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "User satisfying both requirements should get 200"
   221→        }
   222→    ]
   223→
   224→// ===== US3: Restrict Resource by Role =====
   225→
   226→[<Tests>]
   227→let us3Tests =
   228→    testList "US3 - Restrict Resource by Role" [
   229→        testTask "user in role -> 200" {
   230→            let r = resource "/admin-panel" { name "AdminPanel"; requireRole "Admin"; get simpleHandler }
   231→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ r ] ignore
   232→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/admin-panel" (Some "alice") None (Some "Admin")
   233→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "User in required role should get 200"
   234→        }
   235→
   236→        testTask "user not in role -> 403" {
   237→            let r = resource "/admin-panel" { name "AdminPanel"; requireRole "Admin"; get simpleHandler }
   238→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ r ] ignore
   239→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/admin-panel" (Some "bob") None (Some "User")
   240→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.Forbidden "User not in required role should get 403"
   241→        }
   242→    ]
   243→
   244→// ===== US4: Restrict Resource by Named Policy =====
   245→
   246→[<Tests>]
   247→let us4Tests =
   248→    testList "US4 - Restrict Resource by Named Policy" [
   249→        testTask "user satisfying named policy -> 200" {
   250→            let r = resource "/reports" { name "Reports"; requirePolicy "CanViewReports"; get simpleHandler }
   251→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ r ] (fun services ->
   252→                services.AddAuthorization(fun options ->
   253→                    options.AddPolicy("CanViewReports", fun policy ->
   254→                        policy.RequireClaim("scope", "reports:read") |> ignore)) |> ignore)
   255→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/reports" (Some "alice") (Some "scope=reports:read") None
   256→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "User satisfying policy should get 200"
   257→        }
   258→
   259→        testTask "user not satisfying named policy -> 403" {
   260→            let r = resource "/reports" { name "Reports"; requirePolicy "CanViewReports"; get simpleHandler }
   261→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ r ] (fun services ->
   262→                services.AddAuthorization(fun options ->
   263→                    options.AddPolicy("CanViewReports", fun policy ->
   264→                        policy.RequireClaim("scope", "reports:read") |> ignore)) |> ignore)
   265→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/reports" (Some "alice") (Some "scope=reports:write") None
   266→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.Forbidden "User not satisfying policy should get 403"
   267→        }
   268→    ]
   269→
   270→// ===== US6: Compose Multiple Authorization Requirements =====
   271→
   272→[<Tests>]
   273→let us6Tests =
   274→    testList "US6 - Compose Multiple Authorization Requirements" [
   275→        testTask "requireAuth + requireClaim + requireRole, user satisfies all three -> 200" {
   276→            let r = resource "/sensitive" {
   277→                name "Sensitive"
   278→                requireAuth
   279→                requireClaim "scope" "admin"
   280→                requireRole "Engineering"
   281→                get simpleHandler
   282→            }
   283→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ r ] ignore
   284→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/sensitive" (Some "alice") (Some "scope=admin") (Some "Engineering")
   285→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "User satisfying all requirements should get 200"
   286→        }
   287→
   288→        testTask "requireAuth + requireClaim + requireRole, user satisfies only two -> 403" {
   289→            let r = resource "/sensitive" {
   290→                name "Sensitive"
   291→                requireAuth
   292→                requireClaim "scope" "admin"
   293→                requireRole "Engineering"
   294→                get simpleHandler
   295→            }
   296→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ r ] ignore
   297→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/sensitive" (Some "alice") (Some "scope=admin") (Some "Marketing")
   298→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.Forbidden "User missing one requirement should get 403"
   299→        }
   300→    ]
   301→
   302→// ===== Edge Cases =====
   303→
   304→[<Tests>]
   305→let edgeCaseTests =
   306→    testList "Edge Cases" [
   307→        testTask "unauthenticated user accessing a claim-required resource -> 401 (not 403)" {
   308→            let r = resource "/claim-protected" { name "ClaimProtected"; requireClaim "scope" "admin"; get simpleHandler }
   309→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ r ] ignore
   310→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/claim-protected" None None None
   311→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.Unauthorized "Unauthenticated user should get 401 not 403"
   312→        }
   313→
   314→        testTask "empty claim values list -> requires claim type exists with any value" {
   315→            let r = resource "/any-scope" { name "AnyScope"; requireClaim "scope" []; get simpleHandler }
   316→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ r ] ignore
   317→
   318→            let! (response1: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/any-scope" (Some "alice") (Some "scope=anything") None
   319→            Expect.equal response1.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "User with claim type present (any value) should get 200"
   320→
   321→            let! (response2: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/any-scope" (Some "alice") (Some "otherclaim=value") None
   322→            Expect.equal response2.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.Forbidden "User without claim type should get 403"
   323→        }
   324→
   325→        testTask "multiple claim operations with same claim type but different values -> AND semantics" {
   326→            let r = resource "/multi-scope" {
   327→                name "MultiScope"
   328→                requireClaim "scope" "read"
   329→                requireClaim "scope" "write"
   330→                get simpleHandler
   331→            }
   332→            let client = createAuthTestServer [ r ] ignore
   333→
   334→            let! (response1: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/multi-scope" (Some "alice") (Some "scope=read") None
   335→            Expect.equal response1.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.Forbidden "User with only one scope value should get 403"
   336→
   337→            let! (response2: HttpResponseMessage) = sendRequest client HttpMethod.Get "/multi-scope" (Some "alice") (Some "scope=read;scope=write") None
   338→            Expect.equal response2.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "User with both scope values should get 200"
   339→        }
   340→    ]
   341→

## Comprehensive Summary: Branch 001-semantic-resources-phase1-WP12

This branch introduces two major new subsystems to the Frank project:

### 1. NEW PROJECTS/MODULES

**Three new top-level projects:**

1. **Frank.Cli** (`src/Frank.Cli/`)
   - Console application entry point (net10.0, single target)
   - Uses System.CommandLine for CLI parsing
   - Distributabel as dotnet-tool (`frank-cli`)

2. **Frank.Cli.Core** (`src/Frank.Cli.Core/`)
   - Core library (net10.0 single target)
   - ~3000 LOC across 18 F# modules
   - Depends on: FSharp.Compiler.Service, Ionide.ProjInfo, dotNetRdf (Core, Ontology, SHACL)

3. **Frank.LinkedData** (`src/Frank.LinkedData/`)
   - Runtime library (multi-target: net8.0/net9.0/net10.0)
   - ~400 LOC
   - Depends on: Frank, dotNetRdf.Core, ASP.NET Core

**Sample application:**
- **Frank.LinkedData.Sample** with seed data and full CRUD operations demonstrating linked data content negotiation

### 2. OVERALL ARCHITECTURE

The system has two complementary pipelines:

**Pipeline A: Static Extraction (frank-cli)**
```
F# Source Code (AST)
    ↓
[AstAnalyzer]       → Extract resource definitions (@resource CE)
    ↓
[TypeAnalyzer]      → Extract F# type definitions (records, DUs)
    ↓
[TypeMapper]        → F# types → OWL Classes + Properties
[RouteMapper]       → Frank routes → Hydra Resources
[CapabilityMapper]  → HTTP methods → Operations
    ↓
[ShapeGenerator]    → SHACL node shapes from F# types
[VocabularyAligner] → Align with schema.org, other vocabularies
    ↓
[ExtractionState]   → Persist to JSON (ontology + shapes as Turtle)
```

**Pipeline B: Runtime Content Negotiation (Frank.LinkedData)**
```
HTTP Request (Accept header)
    ↓
[LinkedDataMiddleware] → Negotiate RDF media type (turtle/json-ld/rdf+xml)
    ↓
Handler returns JSON
    ↓
[InstanceProjector]  → Project JSON object → RDF instance triples
    ↓
[JsonLdFormatter]    → Serialize IGraph to requested RDF format
[TurtleFormatter]
[RdfXmlFormatter]
    ↓
HTTP Response (RDF)
```

### 3. KEY TYPES AND PATTERNS

**Analysis Types:**
- `AnalyzedResource`: Route + HTTP methods + linkedData flag + location
- `AnalyzedType`: F# type name + fields (Kind: Primitive | Optional | Collection | Reference)
- `AnalyzedField`: Property name + kind + location

**Mapping Types:**
- `MappedClass`: ClassUri + Label + Properties + SuperClasses
- `MappedProperty`: PropertyUri + Domain + Range + IsObjectProperty flag
- `MappedResource`: ResourceUri + RouteTemplate + LinkedClass reference

**State Types:**
- `ExtractionState`: Ontology graph + Shapes graph + SourceMap + Clarifications + Metadata
- `ExtractionMetadata`: Timestamp + SourceHash + ToolVersion + BaseUri + Vocabularies
- `UnmappedType`: TypeName + Reason + Location (for diagnostics)

**RDF Helpers:**
- `RdfNode` discriminated union: UriNode | LiteralNode | BlankNode
- Graph helper functions: createGraph, createUriNode, createLiteralNode, assertTriple
- Vocabulary modules: Rdf, Rdfs, Owl, Shacl, Hydra, SchemaOrg, Xsd

**Extensibility:**
- `ExtractPipeline` record: dependency injection for all extraction steps
- `LinkedDataMarker` metadata tag on endpoints to enable content negotiation
- `useLinkedData` / `useLinkedDataWith` custom operations on WebHostSpec

### 4. HOW THE PIECES CONNECT

**Compile-Time (CLI Tool):**
1. CLI entry point dispatches to commands (extract, clarify, validate, diff, compile)
2. Extract command loads .fsproj, parses F# sources
3. AstAnalyzer walks syntax trees for `resource { }` expressions
4. TypeAnalyzer uses FSharp.Compiler.Service for deep type info
5. TypeMapper generates OWL ontology graph from types
6. RouteMapper generates Hydra resource descriptions with linked class hints
7. CapabilityMapper maps HTTP verbs to Hydra operations
8. ShapeGenerator creates SHACL node shapes for validation
9. VocabularyAligner matches local properties to schema.org URIs
10. State persists to JSON with graphs encoded as Turtle

**Runtime (Linked Data Support):**
1. Application startup: `useLinkedData` loads LinkedDataConfig (ontology + shapes) from assembly
2. Request arrives with Accept header (e.g., `application/ld+json`)
3. Middleware intercepts response for endpoints with `linkedData` marker
4. Handler produces JSON (standard REST response)
5. InstanceProjector maps JSON properties → ontology property URIs
6. Formatters serialize RDF graph to requested media type
7. Response negotiated content-type and RDF serialization returned

**Integration Points:**
- `ResourceBuilderExtensions`: `.linkedData` custom operation to mark endpoints
- `WebHostBuilderExtensions`: `.useLinkedData` to enable middleware + DI
- Frank's metadata system carries `LinkedDataMarker` through endpoint construction
- Assembly reflection loads embedded RDF graphs (ontology + shapes) at startup

### Test Coverage
- 75 new files, mostly test projects (Frank.Cli.Core.Tests, Frank.LinkedData.Tests, Frank.LinkedData.Sample.Tests, Frank.Cli.IntegrationTests)
- Tests for each extraction module, formatter, output format, state persistence, and end-to-end pipeline
- Integration tests verify frank-cli sample extraction workflow

This is a sophisticated semantic web integration adding OWL ontology derivation and SHACL shape generation to Frank, plus runtime RDF content negotiation for HTTP resources.

1→module Program
     2→
     3→open Expecto
     4→
     5→[<EntryPoint>]
     6→let main args =
     7→    runTestsInAssemblyWithCLIArgs [] args
     8→

1→namespace Frank.Cli.Core.Extraction
     2→
     3→open System
     4→open VDS.RDF
     5→open Frank.Cli.Core.Rdf
     6→open Frank.Cli.Core.Rdf.Vocabularies
     7→open Frank.Cli.Core.Analysis
     8→
     9→/// Maps Frank routes to RDF resource identities.
    10→module RouteMapper =
    11→
    12→    type MappedResource = {
    13→        ResourceUri: Uri
    14→        RouteTemplate: string
    15→        UriTemplate: string
    16→        Name: string option
    17→        LinkedClass: Uri option
    18→    }
    19→
    20→    let private routeToSlug (route: string) =
    21→        route.TrimStart('/').Replace("/", "_").Replace("{", "").Replace("}", "")
    22→
    23→    let private resourceUri (config: TypeMapper.MappingConfig) (route: string) =
    24→        let base' = config.BaseUri.ToString().TrimEnd('/')
    25→        let cleanRoute = route.TrimStart('/')
    26→        Uri(base' + "/resources/" + cleanRoute)
    27→
    28→    let private uriTemplate (config: TypeMapper.MappingConfig) (route: string) =
    29→        let base' = config.BaseUri.ToString().TrimEnd('/')
    30→        let cleanRoute = route.TrimStart('/')
    31→        base' + "/resources/" + cleanRoute
    32→
    33→    let private findLinkedClass (config: TypeMapper.MappingConfig) (resource: AnalyzedResource) (types: AnalyzedType list) : Uri option =
    34→        if not resource.HasLinkedData then None
    35→        else
    36→            // Try to match resource name or route segment to a type
    37→            let candidateName =
    38→                resource.Name
    39→                |> Option.defaultWith (fun () ->
    40→                    let parts = resource.RouteTemplate.TrimStart('/').Split('/')
    41→                    parts |> Array.tryHead |> Option.defaultValue "")
    42→            types
    43→            |> List.tryFind (fun t ->
    44→                t.ShortName.ToLowerInvariant() = candidateName.ToLowerInvariant()
    45→                || t.ShortName.ToLowerInvariant() + "s" = candidateName.ToLowerInvariant())
    46→            |> Option.map (fun t ->
    47→                Uri(config.BaseUri.ToString().TrimEnd('/') + "/types/" + t.ShortName))
    48→
    49→    let mapRoutes (config: TypeMapper.MappingConfig) (resources: AnalyzedResource list) (types: AnalyzedType list) : IGraph =
    50→        let graph = createGraph ()
    51→        let rdfType = createUriNode graph (Uri Rdf.Type)
    52→
    53→        for res in resources do
    54→            let resUri = resourceUri config res.RouteTemplate
    55→            let resNode = createUriNode graph resUri
    56→            let template = uriTemplate config res.RouteTemplate
    57→
    58→            // rdf:type hydra:Resource
    59→            assertTriple graph (resNode, rdfType, createUriNode graph (Uri Hydra.Resource))
    60→
    61→            // rdfs:label
    62→            let label = res.Name |> Option.defaultValue res.RouteTemplate
    63→            assertTriple graph (resNode, createUriNode graph (Uri Rdfs.Label), createLiteralNode graph label None)
    64→
    65→            // hydra:template
    66→            assertTriple graph (
    67→                resNode,
    68→                createUriNode graph (Uri Hydra.Template),
    69→                createLiteralNode graph template (Some(Uri Xsd.String)))
    70→
    71→            // hydra:supportedClass if linked
    72→            let linkedClass = findLinkedClass config res types
    73→            linkedClass |> Option.iter (fun classUri ->
    74→                assertTriple graph (resNode, createUriNode graph (Uri Hydra.SupportedClass), createUriNode graph classUri))
    75→
    76→        graph
    77→

1→namespace Frank.Cli.Core.Extraction
     2→
     3→open System
     4→open VDS.RDF
     5→open Frank.Cli.Core.Rdf
     6→open Frank.Cli.Core.Rdf.Vocabularies
     7→open Frank.Cli.Core.Analysis
     8→
     9→/// Generates SHACL shapes from F# constraints.
    10→module ShapeGenerator =
    11→
    12→    let private shapeUri (config: TypeMapper.MappingConfig) (typeName: string) =
    13→        Uri(config.BaseUri.ToString().TrimEnd('/') + "/shapes/" + typeName + "Shape")
    14→
    15→    let private classUri (config: TypeMapper.MappingConfig) (typeName: string) =
    16→        Uri(config.BaseUri.ToString().TrimEnd('/') + "/types/" + typeName)
    17→
    18→    let private propertyUri (config: TypeMapper.MappingConfig) (typeName: string) (fieldName: string) =
    19→        Uri(config.BaseUri.ToString().TrimEnd('/') + "/properties/" + typeName + "/" + fieldName)
    20→
    21→    let rec private fieldKindToRange (config: TypeMapper.MappingConfig) (kind: FieldKind) : Uri * bool =
    22→        match kind with
    23→        | Primitive xsdType ->
    24→            let rangeUri =
    25→                match xsdType with
    26→                | "xsd:string" -> Uri Xsd.String
    27→                | "xsd:integer" -> Uri Xsd.Integer
    28→                | "xsd:long" -> Uri Xsd.Integer
    29→                | "xsd:double" | "xsd:float" -> Uri Xsd.Double
    30→                | "xsd:boolean" -> Uri Xsd.Boolean
    31→                | "xsd:dateTime" -> Uri Xsd.DateTime
    32→                | _ -> Uri Xsd.String
    33→            rangeUri, false
    34→        | Optional inner -> fieldKindToRange config inner
    35→        | Collection element -> fieldKindToRange config element
    36→        | Reference typeName -> classUri config typeName, true
    37→
    38→    let private fieldMinCount (kind: FieldKind) : int =
    39→        match kind with
    40→        | Primitive _ -> 1
    41→        | Optional _ -> 0
    42→        | Collection _ -> 0
    43→        | Reference _ -> 1
    44→
    45→    let private assertPropertyShape
    46→        (graph: IGraph)
    47→        (config: TypeMapper.MappingConfig)
    48→        (shapeNode: INode)
    49→        (typeName: string)
    50→        (field: AnalyzedField)
    51→        (index: int)
    52→        =
    53→        let blankId = sprintf "ps_%s_%s_%d" typeName field.Name index
    54→        let psNode = createBlankNode graph blankId
    55→
    56→        // Link shape to property shape
    57→        assertTriple graph (shapeNode, createUriNode graph (Uri Shacl.Property), psNode)
    58→
    59→        // sh:path
    60→        let propUri = propertyUri config typeName field.Name
    61→        assertTriple graph (psNode, createUriNode graph (Uri Shacl.Path), createUriNode graph propUri)
    62→
    63→        // sh:datatype or sh:class
    64→        let range, isObj = fieldKindToRange config field.Kind
    65→        if isObj then
    66→            assertTriple graph (psNode, createUriNode graph (Uri Shacl.Class), createUriNode graph range)
    67→        else
    68→            assertTriple graph (psNode, createUriNode graph (Uri Shacl.Datatype), createUriNode graph range)
    69→
    70→        // sh:minCount
    71→        let minCount = fieldMinCount field.Kind
    72→        assertTriple graph (
    73→            psNode,
    74→            createUriNode graph (Uri Shacl.MinCount),
    75→            createLiteralNode graph (string minCount) (Some(Uri Xsd.Integer)))
    76→
    77→    let private generateShapeForType (graph: IGraph) (config: TypeMapper.MappingConfig) (analyzedType: AnalyzedType) =
    78→        let fields =
    79→            match analyzedType.Kind with
    80→            | Record fields -> [(analyzedType.ShortName, fields)]
    81→            | DiscriminatedUnion cases ->
    82→                // Shape for each case with fields
    83→                cases
    84→                |> List.filter (fun c -> not c.Fields.IsEmpty)
    85→                |> List.map (fun c -> (c.Name, c.Fields))
    86→                |> fun caseShapes ->
    87→                    // Also a shape for the union type itself (no properties)
    88→                    (analyzedType.ShortName, []) :: caseShapes
    89→            | Enum _ -> [(analyzedType.ShortName, [])]
    90→
    91→        for (typeName, typeFields) in fields do
    92→            let sUri = shapeUri config typeName
    93→            let sNode = createUriNode graph sUri
    94→            let rdfType = createUriNode graph (Uri Rdf.Type)
    95→
    96→            // rdf:type sh:NodeShape
    97→            assertTriple graph (sNode, rdfType, createUriNode graph (Uri Shacl.NodeShape))
    98→
    99→            // sh:targetClass
   100→            let cUri = classUri config typeName
   101→            assertTriple graph (sNode, createUriNode graph (Uri Shacl.TargetClass), createUriNode graph cUri)
   102→
   103→            // Property shapes
   104→            typeFields |> List.iteri (fun i field ->
   105→                assertPropertyShape graph config sNode typeName field i)
   106→
   107→    let generateShapes (config: TypeMapper.MappingConfig) (types: AnalyzedType list) : IGraph =
   108→        let graph = createGraph ()
   109→        for t in types do
   110→            generateShapeForType graph config t
   111→        graph
   112→

1→namespace Frank.Cli.Core.Extraction
     2→
     3→open System
     4→open VDS.RDF
     5→open Frank.Cli.Core.Rdf
     6→open Frank.Cli.Core.Rdf.Vocabularies
     7→open Frank.Cli.Core.Analysis
     8→
     9→/// Maps F# types (DUs, records) to OWL classes and properties.
    10→module TypeMapper =
    11→
    12→    type MappingConfig = {
    13→        BaseUri: Uri
    14→        Vocabularies: string list
    15→    }
    16→
    17→    type MappedProperty = {
    18→        PropertyUri: Uri
    19→        Label: string
    20→        Domain: Uri
    21→        Range: Uri
    22→        IsObjectProperty: bool
    23→    }
    24→
    25→    type MappedClass = {
    26→        ClassUri: Uri
    27→        Label: string
    28→        Properties: MappedProperty list
    29→        SuperClasses: Uri list
    30→    }
    31→
    32→    let private classUri (config: MappingConfig) (typeName: string) =
    33→        Uri(config.BaseUri.ToString().TrimEnd('/') + "/types/" + typeName)
    34→
    35→    let private propertyUri (config: MappingConfig) (typeName: string) (fieldName: string) =
    36→        Uri(config.BaseUri.ToString().TrimEnd('/') + "/properties/" + typeName + "/" + fieldName)
    37→
    38→    let rec private fieldKindToRange (config: MappingConfig) (kind: FieldKind) : Uri * bool =
    39→        match kind with
    40→        | Primitive xsdType ->
    41→            let rangeUri =
    42→                match xsdType with
    43→                | "xsd:string" -> Uri Xsd.String
    44→                | "xsd:integer" -> Uri Xsd.Integer
    45→                | "xsd:long" -> Uri Xsd.Integer
    46→                | "xsd:double" | "xsd:float" -> Uri Xsd.Double
    47→                | "xsd:boolean" -> Uri Xsd.Boolean
    48→                | "xsd:dateTime" -> Uri Xsd.DateTime
    49→                | _ -> Uri Xsd.String
    50→            rangeUri, false
    51→        | Optional inner -> fieldKindToRange config inner
    52→        | Collection element -> fieldKindToRange config element
    53→        | Reference typeName -> classUri config typeName, true
    54→
    55→    let private mapField (config: MappingConfig) (typeName: string) (field: AnalyzedField) : MappedProperty =
    56→        let range, isObj = fieldKindToRange config field.Kind
    57→        {
    58→            PropertyUri = propertyUri config typeName field.Name
    59→            Label = field.Name
    60→            Domain = classUri config typeName
    61→            Range = range
    62→            IsObjectProperty = isObj
    63→        }
    64→
    65→    let private assertProperty (graph: IGraph) (prop: MappedProperty) =
    66→        let propNode = createUriNode graph prop.PropertyUri
    67→        let rdfType = createUriNode graph (Uri Rdf.Type)
    68→
    69→        // rdf:type owl:DatatypeProperty or owl:ObjectProperty
    70→        let propTypeUri =
    71→            if prop.IsObjectProperty then Uri Owl.ObjectProperty
    72→            else Uri Owl.DatatypeProperty
    73→        assertTriple graph (propNode, rdfType, createUriNode graph propTypeUri)
    74→
    75→        // rdfs:domain
    76→        assertTriple graph (propNode, createUriNode graph (Uri Rdfs.Domain), createUriNode graph prop.Domain)
    77→
    78→        // rdfs:range
    79→        assertTriple graph (propNode, createUriNode graph (Uri Rdfs.Range), createUriNode graph prop.Range)
    80→
    81→        // rdfs:label
    82→        assertTriple graph (propNode, createUriNode graph (Uri Rdfs.Label), createLiteralNode graph prop.Label None)
    83→
    84→    let private assertClass (graph: IGraph) (cls: MappedClass) =
    85→        let classNode = createUriNode graph cls.ClassUri
    86→        let rdfType = createUriNode graph (Uri Rdf.Type)
    87→
    88→        // rdf:type owl:Class
    89→        assertTriple graph (classNode, rdfType, createUriNode graph (Uri Owl.Class))
    90→
    91→        // rdfs:label
    92→        assertTriple graph (classNode, createUriNode graph (Uri Rdfs.Label), createLiteralNode graph cls.Label None)
    93→
    94→        // rdfs:subClassOf
    95→        for superClass in cls.SuperClasses do
    96→            assertTriple graph (classNode, createUriNode graph (Uri Rdfs.SubClassOf), createUriNode graph superClass)
    97→
    98→        // Assert all properties
    99→        for prop in cls.Properties do
   100→            assertProperty graph prop
   101→
   102→    let private mapAnalyzedType (config: MappingConfig) (analyzedType: AnalyzedType) : MappedClass list =
   103→        match analyzedType.Kind with
   104→        | Record fields ->
   105→            let props = fields |> List.map (mapField config analyzedType.ShortName)
   106→            [{
   107→                ClassUri = classUri config analyzedType.ShortName
   108→                Label = analyzedType.ShortName
   109→                Properties = props
   110→                SuperClasses = []
   111→            }]
   112→        | DiscriminatedUnion cases ->
   113→            let parentUri = classUri config analyzedType.ShortName
   114→            let parentClass = {
   115→                ClassUri = parentUri
   116→                Label = analyzedType.ShortName
   117→                Properties = []
   118→                SuperClasses = []
   119→            }
   120→            let caseClasses =
   121→                cases |> List.map (fun case ->
   122→                    let caseProps = case.Fields |> List.map (mapField config case.Name)
   123→                    {
   124→                        ClassUri = classUri config case.Name
   125→                        Label = case.Name
   126→                        Properties = caseProps
   127→                        SuperClasses = [parentUri]
   128→                    })
   129→            parentClass :: caseClasses
   130→        | Enum values ->
   131→            [{
   132→                ClassUri = classUri config analyzedType.ShortName
   133→                Label = analyzedType.ShortName
   134→                Properties = []
   135→                SuperClasses = []
   136→            }]
   137→
   138→    let mapTypes (config: MappingConfig) (types: AnalyzedType list) : IGraph =
   139→        let graph = createGraph ()
   140→        let allClasses = types |> List.collect (mapAnalyzedType config)
   141→        for cls in allClasses do
   142→            assertClass graph cls
   143→        graph
   144→

    62→            let second = def.Produces.[1]

    65→
    66→        test "handler with producesEmpty operation for no-content responses" {

    74→            Expect.hasLength def.Produces 2 "Should have 2 produces entries"
    75→
    76→            let first = def.Produces.[0]
    77→            Expect.equal first.StatusCode 204 "First status code should be 204"
    78→            Expect.isNone first.ResponseType "First response type should be None"
    79→            Expect.isEmpty first.ContentTypes "First content types should be empty"
    80→            Expect.isNone first.Description "First description should be None"
    81→
    82→            let second = def.Produces.[1]

    86→
    87→        test "handler with accepts operation populates Accepts list" {

    95→            Expect.hasLength def.Accepts 2 "Should have 2 accepts entries"

    97→            let first = def.Accepts.[0]

    99→            Expect.equal first.ContentTypes [ "application/json" ] "First content types should be default"

   102→            let second = def.Accepts.[1]

   177→
   178→            Expect.hasLength def.Accepts 1 "Should have 1 accepts entry"
