1→namespace Frank.Cli.Core.Extraction
     2→
     3→open System
     4→open System.Text.RegularExpressions
     5→open VDS.RDF
     6→open Frank.Cli.Core.Rdf
     7→open Frank.Cli.Core.Rdf.Vocabularies
     8→
     9→/// Aligns extracted RDF to standard vocabularies (Schema.org, Hydra).
    10→module VocabularyAligner =
    11→
    12→    let private splitCamelCase (name: string) =
    13→        Regex.Replace(name, "([a-z])([A-Z])", "$1 $2").ToLowerInvariant()
    14→
    15→    let private normalizeFieldName (name: string) =
    16→        splitCamelCase(name).Replace(" ", "").ToLowerInvariant()
    17→
    18→    let private alignmentMap : (string list * string) list = [
    19→        (["name"; "title"], SchemaOrg.Name)
    20→        (["description"; "summary"; "body"], SchemaOrg.Description)
    21→        (["email"; "emailaddress"], SchemaOrg.Email)
    22→        (["url"; "uri"; "website"; "homepage"], SchemaOrg.Url)
    23→        (["price"; "cost"; "amount"], SchemaOrg.Price)
    24→        (["createdat"; "datecreated"; "created"], SchemaOrg.DateCreated)
    25→        (["updatedat"; "datemodified"; "modified"], SchemaOrg.DateModified)
    26→        (["image"; "imageurl"; "photo"], SchemaOrg.Image)
    27→        (["telephone"; "phone"], SchemaOrg.Telephone)
    28→    ]
    29→
    30→    let private tryFindAlignment (fieldName: string) : string option =
    31→        let normalized = normalizeFieldName fieldName
    32→        alignmentMap
    33→        |> List.tryFind (fun (names, _) -> names |> List.contains normalized)
    34→        |> Option.map snd
    35→
    36→    let alignVocabularies (config: TypeMapper.MappingConfig) (graph: IGraph) : IGraph =
    37→        if not (config.Vocabularies |> List.contains "schema.org") then
    38→            graph
    39→        else
    40→            // Find all triples where the predicate is rdfs:label and subject is a property
    41→            // (i.e., subject has rdf:type owl:DatatypeProperty or owl:ObjectProperty)
    42→            let rdfTypeUri = Uri Rdf.Type
    43→            let datatypePropUri = Uri Owl.DatatypeProperty
    44→            let objectPropUri = Uri Owl.ObjectProperty
    45→            let equivalentPropUri = Uri Owl.EquivalentProperty
    46→
    47→            let rdfTypeNode = createUriNode graph rdfTypeUri
    48→            let labelNode = createUriNode graph (Uri Rdfs.Label)
    49→
    50→            // Collect all property nodes (nodes that are typed as owl:DatatypeProperty or owl:ObjectProperty)
    51→            let propertyNodes =
    52→                triplesWithPredicate graph rdfTypeNode
    53→                |> Seq.filter (fun t ->
    54→                    match t.Object with
    55→                    | :? IUriNode as on ->
    56→                        on.Uri = datatypePropUri || on.Uri = objectPropUri
    57→                    | _ -> false)
    58→                |> Seq.map (fun t -> t.Subject)
    59→                |> Seq.distinct
    60→                |> Seq.toList
    61→
    62→            // For each property node, find its rdfs:label and try to align
    63→            for propNode in propertyNodes do
    64→                let labelTriples =
    65→                    triplesWithSubjectPredicate graph propNode labelNode
    66→                    |> Seq.toList
    67→
    68→                for labelTriple in labelTriples do
    69→                    match labelTriple.Object with
    70→                    | :? ILiteralNode as lit ->
    71→                        match tryFindAlignment lit.Value with
    72→                        | Some schemaUri ->
    73→                            let equivNode = createUriNode graph equivalentPropUri
    74→                            let schemaNode = createUriNode graph (Uri schemaUri)
    75→                            assertTriple graph (propNode, equivNode, schemaNode)
    76→                        | None -> ()
    77→                    | _ -> ()
    78→
    79→            graph
    80→

1→module Frank.OpenApi.Tests.MetadataTests
     2→
     3→open System
     4→open System.Threading.Tasks
     5→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http
     6→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.Metadata
     7→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing
     8→open Expecto
     9→open Frank.Builder
    10→open Frank.OpenApi
    11→
    12→// Sample types for testing
    13→type Product = { Name: string; Price: decimal }
    14→type CreateRequest = { Name: string }
    15→
    16→[<Tests>]
    17→let tests =
    18→    testList "Metadata Integration" [
    19→        test "HandlerDefinition passed to resource get operation adds metadata to endpoint" {
    20→            let handlerDef =
    21→                handler {
    22→                    name "getProducts"
    23→                    summary "List products"
    24→                    description "Returns all products"
    25→                    tags [ "Products"; "Public" ]
    26→                    produces typeof<Product> 200
    27→                    handle (fun (ctx: HttpContext) -> Task.CompletedTask)
    28→                }
    29→
    30→            let builtResource =
    31→                resource "/products" {
    32→                    name "Products"
    33→                    get handlerDef
    34→                }
    35→
    36→            Expect.hasLength builtResource.Endpoints 1 "Should have 1 endpoint"
    37→
    38→            let endpoint = builtResource.Endpoints.[0]
    39→
    40→            // Check endpoint name metadata
    41→            let nameMetadata = endpoint.Metadata.GetMetadata<EndpointNameMetadata>()
    42→            Expect.isNotNull nameMetadata "Should have name metadata"
    43→            Expect.equal nameMetadata.EndpointName "getProducts" "Endpoint name should match"
    44→
    45→            // Check summary
    46→            let summaryMetadata = endpoint.Metadata.GetMetadata<EndpointSummaryAttribute>()
    47→            Expect.isNotNull summaryMetadata "Should have summary metadata"
    48→            Expect.equal summaryMetadata.Summary "List products" "Summary should match"
    49→
    50→            // Check description
    51→            let descMetadata = endpoint.Metadata.GetMetadata<EndpointDescriptionAttribute>()
    52→            Expect.isNotNull descMetadata "Should have description metadata"
    53→            Expect.equal descMetadata.Description "Returns all products" "Description should match"
    54→
    55→            // Check tags
    56→            let tagsMetadata = endpoint.Metadata.GetMetadata<TagsAttribute>()
    57→            Expect.isNotNull tagsMetadata "Should have tags metadata"
    58→            Expect.containsAll tagsMetadata.Tags [ "Products"; "Public" ] "Tags should match"
    59→
    60→            // Check produces response type
    61→            let producesMetadata = endpoint.Metadata.GetMetadata<ProducesResponseTypeMetadata>()
    62→            Expect.isNotNull producesMetadata "Should have produces metadata"
    63→        }
    64→
    65→        test "HandlerDefinition with accepts metadata adds request body metadata" {
    66→            let handlerDef =
    67→                handler {
    68→                    accepts typeof<CreateRequest>
    69→                    handle (fun (ctx: HttpContext) -> Task.CompletedTask)
    70→                }
    71→
    72→            let builtResource =
    73→                resource "/products" {
    74→                    post handlerDef
    75→                }
    76→
    77→            let endpoint = builtResource.Endpoints.[0]
    78→
    79→            let acceptsMetadata = endpoint.Metadata.GetMetadata<AcceptsMetadata>()
    80→            Expect.isNotNull acceptsMetadata "Should have accepts metadata"
    81→            Expect.equal acceptsMetadata.RequestType typeof<CreateRequest> "Request type should match"
    82→            Expect.contains acceptsMetadata.ContentTypes "application/json" "Should have JSON content type"
    83→            Expect.isFalse acceptsMetadata.IsOptional "Should not be optional"
    84→        }
    85→
    86→        test "Resource with plain handler and HandlerDefinition both work" {
    87→            let plainHandler : RequestDelegate = RequestDelegate(fun ctx -> Task.CompletedTask)
    88→
    89→            let handlerDef =
    90→                handler {
    91→                    name "createProduct"
    92→                    produces typeof<Product> 201
    93→                    handle (fun (ctx: HttpContext) -> Task.CompletedTask)
    94→                }
    95→
    96→            let builtResource =
    97→                resource "/products" {
    98→                    get plainHandler
    99→                    post handlerDef
   100→                }
   101→
   102→            Expect.hasLength builtResource.Endpoints 2 "Should have 2 endpoints"
   103→
   104→            // Find endpoints by HTTP method
   105→            let getEndpoint =
   106→                builtResource.Endpoints
   107→                |> Array.find (fun e ->
   108→                    let meta = e.Metadata.GetMetadata<HttpMethodMetadata>()
   109→                    meta <> null && (meta.HttpMethods |> Seq.contains "GET"))
   110→
   111→            let postEndpoint =
   112→                builtResource.Endpoints
   113→                |> Array.find (fun e ->
   114→                    let meta = e.Metadata.GetMetadata<HttpMethodMetadata>()
   115→                    meta <> null && (meta.HttpMethods |> Seq.contains "POST"))
   116→
   117→            // Plain handler should have HttpMethodMetadata but no name metadata
   118→            let getHttpMethod = getEndpoint.Metadata.GetMetadata<HttpMethodMetadata>()
   119→            Expect.isNotNull getHttpMethod "GET should have HTTP method metadata"
   120→            let getName = getEndpoint.Metadata.GetMetadata<EndpointNameMetadata>()
   121→            Expect.isNull getName "GET should not have name metadata"
   122→
   123→            // HandlerDefinition should have both HttpMethodMetadata and name metadata
   124→            let postHttpMethod = postEndpoint.Metadata.GetMetadata<HttpMethodMetadata>()
   125→            Expect.isNotNull postHttpMethod "POST should have HTTP method metadata"
   126→            let postName = postEndpoint.Metadata.GetMetadata<EndpointNameMetadata>()
   127→            Expect.isNotNull postName "POST should have name metadata"
   128→            Expect.equal postName.EndpointName "createProduct" "POST name should match"
   129→        }
   130→
   131→        test "HandlerDefinition works with all HTTP methods" {
   132→            let createHandler methodName =
   133→                handler {
   134→                    name methodName
   135→                    handle (fun (ctx: HttpContext) -> Task.CompletedTask)
   136→                }
   137→
   138→            let builtResource =
   139→                resource "/test" {
   140→                    get (createHandler "testGet")
   141→                    post (createHandler "testPost")
   142→                    put (createHandler "testPut")
   143→                    delete (createHandler "testDelete")
   144→                    patch (createHandler "testPatch")
   145→                }
   146→
   147→            Expect.hasLength builtResource.Endpoints 5 "Should have 5 endpoints"
   148→
   149→            // Verify each endpoint has the correct name
   150→            let endpoints = builtResource.Endpoints |> Array.toList
   151→            let names = endpoints |> List.map (fun e ->
   152→                let meta = e.Metadata.GetMetadata<EndpointNameMetadata>()
   153→                if isNull meta then null else meta.EndpointName)
   154→
   155→            Expect.contains names "testGet" "Should have GET endpoint"
   156→            Expect.contains names "testPost" "Should have POST endpoint"
   157→            Expect.contains names "testPut" "Should have PUT endpoint"
   158→            Expect.contains names "testDelete" "Should have DELETE endpoint"
   159→            Expect.contains names "testPatch" "Should have PATCH endpoint"
   160→        }
   161→
   162→        test "HandlerDefinition with multiple produces entries creates multiple metadata entries" {
   163→            let handlerDef =
   164→                handler {
   165→                    produces typeof<Product> 200
   166→                    produces typeof<Product> 201
   167→                    producesEmpty 404
   168→                    handle (fun (ctx: HttpContext) -> Task.CompletedTask)
   169→                }
   170→
   171→            let builtResource =
   172→                resource "/products" {
   173→                    get handlerDef
   174→                }
   175→
   176→            let endpoint = builtResource.Endpoints.[0]
   177→
   178→            // Count ProducesResponseTypeMetadata instances
   179→            let producesMetadata =
   180→                endpoint.Metadata
   181→                |> Seq.filter (fun m -> m :? ProducesResponseTypeMetadata)
   182→                |> Seq.cast<ProducesResponseTypeMetadata>
   183→                |> Seq.toList
   184→
   185→            Expect.hasLength producesMetadata 3 "Should have 3 produces metadata entries"
   186→
   187→            let statusCodes = producesMetadata |> List.map (fun m -> m.StatusCode) |> List.sort
   188→            Expect.equal statusCodes [ 200; 201; 404 ] "Should have all status codes"
   189→        }
   190→
   191→        test "HandlerDefinition with custom content types" {
   192→            let handlerDef =
   193→                handler {
   194→                    produces typeof<Product> 200 ["application/xml"; "application/json"]
   195→                    accepts typeof<CreateRequest> ["application/xml"]
   196→                    handle (fun (ctx: HttpContext) -> Task.CompletedTask)
   197→                }
   198→
   199→            let builtResource =
   200→                resource "/products" {
   201→                    post handlerDef
   202→                }
   203→
   204→            let endpoint = builtResource.Endpoints.[0]
   205→
   206→            let producesMetadata = endpoint.Metadata.GetMetadata<ProducesResponseTypeMetadata>()
   207→            Expect.isNotNull producesMetadata "Should have produces metadata"
   208→            Expect.containsAll producesMetadata.ContentTypes ["application/xml"; "application/json"] "Should have custom content types"
   209→
   210→            let acceptsMetadata = endpoint.Metadata.GetMetadata<AcceptsMetadata>()
   211→            Expect.isNotNull acceptsMetadata "Should have accepts metadata"
   212→            Expect.contains acceptsMetadata.ContentTypes "application/xml" "Should accept XML"
   213→        }
   214→    ]
   215→

1→namespace Frank.Cli.Core.Commands
     2→
     3→open System
     4→open System.IO
     5→open VDS.RDF
     6→open Frank.Cli.Core.Rdf
     7→open Frank.Cli.Core.Rdf.Vocabularies
     8→open Frank.Cli.Core.State
     9→
    10→/// Ambiguity detection command: identifies unmapped types and routes.
    11→module ClarifyCommand =
    12→
    13→    type ClarifyQuestion =
    14→        { Id: string
    15→          Category: string
    16→          QuestionText: string
    17→          Context: {| SourceType: string; Location: string option |}
    18→          Options: {| Label: string; Impact: string |} list }
    19→
    20→    type ClarifyResult =
    21→        { Questions: ClarifyQuestion list
    22→          ResolvedCount: int
    23→          TotalCount: int }
    24→
    25→    let private slugify (s: string) =
    26→        s.Replace(".", "-").Replace(" ", "-")
    27→
    28→    let private unmappedTypeQuestions (state: ExtractionState) : ClarifyQuestion list =
    29→        state.UnmappedTypes
    30→        |> List.map (fun ut ->
    31→            let id = $"unmapped-type-%s{slugify ut.TypeName}"
    32→            { Id = id
    33→              Category = "unmapped-type"
    34→              QuestionText = $"Type '%s{ut.TypeName}' was found but could not be mapped. Should it be:"
    35→              Context =
    36→                {| SourceType = ut.TypeName
    37→                   Location = Some $"%s{ut.Location.File}:%d{ut.Location.Line}" |}
    38→              Options =
    39→                [ {| Label = "mapped as owl:Class"; Impact = "Will generate an OWL class definition" |}
    40→                  {| Label = "ignored"; Impact = "Will be excluded from the ontology" |}
    41→                  {| Label = "mapped as a custom vocabulary term"; Impact = "Will be added with a custom namespace" |} ] })
    42→
    43→    let private openOrClosedQuestions (state: ExtractionState) : ClarifyQuestion list =
    44→        // Find classes in the ontology that have subclasses (DU types)
    45→        let graph = state.Ontology
    46→        let rdfTypeNode = createUriNode graph (Uri Rdf.Type)
    47→        let subClassOfNode = createUriNode graph (Uri Rdfs.SubClassOf)
    48→        let owlClassNode = createUriNode graph (Uri Owl.Class)
    49→
    50→        // Find all classes (use AbsoluteUri strings since Uri doesn't support comparison)
    51→        let allClassUris =
    52→            triplesWithPredicate graph rdfTypeNode
    53→            |> Seq.filter (fun t ->
    54→                match t.Object with
    55→                | :? IUriNode as on -> on.Uri = Uri Owl.Class
    56→                | _ -> false)
    57→            |> Seq.choose (fun t ->
    58→                match t.Subject with
    59→                | :? IUriNode as sn -> Some sn.Uri.AbsoluteUri
    60→                | _ -> None)
    61→            |> Set.ofSeq
    62→
    63→        // Find classes that are superclasses (have subClassOf triples pointing to them)
    64→        let superClasses =
    65→            triplesWithPredicate graph subClassOfNode
    66→            |> Seq.choose (fun t ->
    67→                match t.Object with
    68→                | :? IUriNode as on when allClassUris.Contains on.Uri.AbsoluteUri -> Some on.Uri
    69→                | _ -> None)
    70→            |> Seq.distinctBy (fun (u: Uri) -> u.AbsoluteUri)
    71→            |> Seq.toList
    72→
    73→        superClasses
    74→        |> List.map (fun classUri ->
    75→            let typeName = classUri.Segments |> Array.last
    76→            let id = $"open-or-closed-%s{slugify typeName}"
    77→            { Id = id
    78→              Category = "open-or-closed"
    79→              QuestionText = $"Is '%s{typeName}' an open or closed enumeration?"
    80→              Context = {| SourceType = typeName; Location = None |}
    81→              Options =
    82→                [ {| Label = "open (extensible)"; Impact = "New subtypes can be added without breaking compatibility" |}
    83→                  {| Label = "closed (fixed)"; Impact = "The set of subtypes is fixed and complete" |} ] })
    84→
    85→    let private objectOrDatatypeQuestions (state: ExtractionState) : ClarifyQuestion list =
    86→        // Find properties with names like URL/email patterns
    87→        let graph = state.Ontology
    88→        let rdfTypeNode = createUriNode graph (Uri Rdf.Type)
    89→        let labelNode = createUriNode graph (Uri Rdfs.Label)
    90→        let domainNode = createUriNode graph (Uri Rdfs.Domain)
    91→        let datatypePropUri = Uri Owl.DatatypeProperty
    92→
    93→        let urlLikeNames = Set.ofList ["url"; "uri"; "website"; "homepage"; "email"; "emailaddress"; "link"; "href"]
    94→
    95→        // Find datatype properties whose label matches URL-like names
    96→        let datatypeProperties =
    97→            triplesWithPredicate graph rdfTypeNode
    98→            |> Seq.filter (fun t ->
    99→                match t.Object with
   100→                | :? IUriNode as on -> on.Uri = datatypePropUri
   101→                | _ -> false)
   102→            |> Seq.choose (fun t ->
   103→                match t.Subject with
   104→                | :? IUriNode as sn -> Some sn
   105→                | _ -> None)
   106→            |> Seq.toList
   107→
   108→        datatypeProperties
   109→        |> List.choose (fun propNode ->
   110→            let labels =
   111→                triplesWithSubjectPredicate graph (propNode :> INode) labelNode
   112→                |> Seq.choose (fun t ->
   113→                    match t.Object with
   114→                    | :? ILiteralNode as lit -> Some lit.Value
   115→                    | _ -> None)
   116→                |> Seq.toList
   117→
   118→            let matchingLabel =
   119→                labels
   120→                |> List.tryFind (fun l -> urlLikeNames.Contains(l.ToLowerInvariant()))
   121→
   122→            match matchingLabel with
   123→            | None -> None
   124→            | Some fieldName ->
   125→                // Find the domain class
   126→                let domainClass =
   127→                    triplesWithSubjectPredicate graph (propNode :> INode) domainNode
   128→                    |> Seq.choose (fun t ->
   129→                        match t.Object with
   130→                        | :? IUriNode as on -> Some (on.Uri.Segments |> Array.last)
   131→                        | _ -> None)
   132→                    |> Seq.tryHead
   133→                    |> Option.defaultValue "Unknown"
   134→
   135→                Some
   136→                    { Id = $"object-or-datatype-%s{slugify fieldName}-%s{slugify domainClass}"
   137→                      Category = "object-or-datatype"
   138→                      QuestionText = $"Should '%s{fieldName}' on '%s{domainClass}' be an object property (link) or datatype property (literal)?"
   139→                      Context = {| SourceType = domainClass; Location = None |}
   140→                      Options =
   141→                        [ {| Label = "object property (link)"; Impact = "Value will be a URI reference to another resource" |}
   142→                          {| Label = "datatype property (literal)"; Impact = "Value will be a string literal (e.g., URL as text)" |} ] })
   143→
   144→    let private missingRelationshipQuestions (state: ExtractionState) : ClarifyQuestion list =
   145→        // Detect pairs of classes where one type's name appears in the other's fields
   146→        // but no owl:ObjectProperty links them.
   147→        let graph = state.Ontology
   148→        let rdfTypeNode = createUriNode graph (Uri Rdf.Type)
   149→        let objectPropUri = Uri Owl.ObjectProperty
   150→        let domainNode = createUriNode graph (Uri Rdfs.Domain)
   151→        let rangeNode = createUriNode graph (Uri Rdfs.Range)
   152→
   153→        // Collect all class URIs and their short names
   154→        let allClasses =
   155→            triplesWithPredicate graph rdfTypeNode
   156→            |> Seq.filter (fun t ->
   157→                match t.Object with
   158→                | :? IUriNode as on -> on.Uri = Uri Owl.Class
   159→                | _ -> false)
   160→            |> Seq.choose (fun t ->
   161→                match t.Subject with
   162→                | :? IUriNode as sn -> Some (sn.Uri.AbsoluteUri, sn.Uri.Segments |> Array.last)
   163→                | _ -> None)
   164→            |> Seq.toList
   165→
   166→        // Collect all object properties and their domain->range pairs
   167→        let objectPropertyLinks =
   168→            triplesWithPredicate graph rdfTypeNode
   169→            |> Seq.filter (fun t ->
   170→                match t.Object with
   171→                | :? IUriNode as on -> on.Uri = objectPropUri
   172→                | _ -> false)
   173→            |> Seq.choose (fun t ->
   174→                match t.Subject with
   175→                | :? IUriNode as sn -> Some sn
   176→                | _ -> None)
   177→            |> Seq.collect (fun propNode ->
   178→                let domains =
   179→                    triplesWithSubjectPredicate graph (propNode :> INode) domainNode
   180→                    |> Seq.choose (fun t ->
   181→                        match t.Object with
   182→                        | :? IUriNode as on -> Some on.Uri.AbsoluteUri
   183→                        | _ -> None)
   184→                let ranges =
   185→                    triplesWithSubjectPredicate graph (propNode :> INode) rangeNode
   186→                    |> Seq.choose (fun t ->
   187→                        match t.Object with
   188→                        | :? IUriNode as on -> Some on.Uri.AbsoluteUri
   189→                        | _ -> None)
   190→                Seq.allPairs domains ranges)
   191→            |> Set.ofSeq
   192→
   193→        // For each pair of classes, check if one name appears in the other's rdfs:label fields
   194→        // but no object property links them
   195→        let classUriSet = allClasses |> List.map fst |> Set.ofList
   196→
   197→        let questions = ResizeArray<ClarifyQuestion>()
   198→        let seen = System.Collections.Generic.HashSet<string>()
   199→
   200→        for (uriA, nameA) in allClasses do
   201→            for (uriB, nameB) in allClasses do
   202→                if uriA <> uriB then
   203→                    let nameAppearsInB =
   204→                        nameA.Length >= 3 && nameB.ToLowerInvariant().Contains(nameA.ToLowerInvariant())
   205→                    if nameAppearsInB then
   206→                        // Check if any object property links A -> B or B -> A
   207→                        let linked =
   208→                            objectPropertyLinks.Contains(uriA, uriB)
   209→                            || objectPropertyLinks.Contains(uriB, uriA)
   210→                        if not linked then
   211→                            let pairKey = if uriA < uriB then $"{nameA}-{nameB}" else $"{nameB}-{nameA}"
   212→                            if seen.Add(pairKey) then
   213→                                questions.Add(
   214→                                    { Id = $"missing-relationship-%s{slugify nameA}-%s{slugify nameB}"
   215→                                      Category = "missing-relationship"
   216→                                      QuestionText = $"Types '%s{nameA}' and '%s{nameB}' appear related but have no explicit property linking them. Should a relationship be added?"
   217→                                      Context = {| SourceType = nameA; Location = None |}
   218→                                      Options =
   219→                                        [ {| Label = $"add object property from %s{nameA} to %s{nameB}"; Impact = $"Will create an owl:ObjectProperty linking %s{nameA} to %s{nameB}" |}
   220→                                          {| Label = $"add object property from %s{nameB} to %s{nameA}"; Impact = $"Will create an owl:ObjectProperty linking %s{nameB} to %s{nameA}" |}
   221→                                          {| Label = "no relationship needed"; Impact = "Types will remain unlinked in the ontology" |} ] })
   222→
   223→        questions |> Seq.toList
   224→
   225→    let execute (projectPath: string) : Result<ClarifyResult, string> =
   226→        let statePath = ExtractionState.defaultStatePath (Path.GetDirectoryName projectPath)
   227→
   228→        match ExtractionState.load statePath with
   229→        | Error e -> Error $"Failed to load state: {e}"
   230→        | Ok state ->
   231→
   232→        let unmappedQuestions = unmappedTypeQuestions state
   233→        let openClosedQuestions = openOrClosedQuestions state
   234→        let objDatatypeQuestions = objectOrDatatypeQuestions state
   235→        let missingRelQuestions = missingRelationshipQuestions state
   236→
   237→        let allQuestions = unmappedQuestions @ openClosedQuestions @ objDatatypeQuestions @ missingRelQuestions
   238→
   239→        let resolvedCount =
   240→            allQuestions
   241→            |> List.filter (fun q -> state.Clarifications.ContainsKey q.Id)
   242→            |> List.length
   243→
   244→        Ok
   245→            { Questions = allQuestions
   246→              ResolvedCount = resolvedCount
   247→              TotalCount = allQuestions.Length }
   248→

1→module Frank.OpenApi.Tests.OpenApiDocumentTests
     2→
     3→open System.Net
     4→open System.Net.Http
     5→open System.Text.Json
     6→open System.Threading.Tasks
     7→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder
     8→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting
     9→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http
    10→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi
    11→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing
    12→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.TestHost
    13→open Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection
    14→open Microsoft.Extensions.FileProviders
    15→open Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting
    16→open FSharp.Data.JsonSchema.OpenApi
    17→open Expecto
    18→open Frank.Builder
    19→open Frank.OpenApi
    20→open Scalar.AspNetCore
    21→
    22→/// Simple endpoint data source for tests (ResourceEndpointDataSource is internal)
    23→type TestEndpointDataSource(endpoints: Endpoint[]) =
    24→    inherit EndpointDataSource()
    25→    override _.Endpoints = endpoints :> _
    26→    override _.GetChangeToken() = NullChangeToken.Singleton :> _
    27→
    28→/// Creates a test server with Frank resources and OpenAPI enabled
    29→let createOpenApiTestServer (resources: Resource list) =
    30→    let allEndpoints = resources |> List.collect (fun r -> r.Endpoints |> Array.toList) |> List.toArray
    31→    let builder =
    32→        Host.CreateDefaultBuilder([||])
    33→            .ConfigureWebHost(fun webBuilder ->
    34→                webBuilder
    35→                    .UseTestServer()
    36→                    .ConfigureServices(fun services ->
    37→                        services.AddRouting() |> ignore
    38→                        services.AddOpenApi(fun options ->
    39→                            options.AddSchemaTransformer(FSharpSchemaTransformer()) |> ignore
    40→                            options.AddOperationTransformer(fun operation context _ct ->
    41→                                if System.String.IsNullOrEmpty operation.OperationId then
    42→                                    let hasExplicitName =
    43→                                        context.Description.ActionDescriptor.EndpointMetadata
    44→                                        |> Seq.exists (fun m -> m :? EndpointNameMetadata)
    45→                                    if not hasExplicitName then
    46→                                        let displayName = context.Description.ActionDescriptor.DisplayName
    47→                                        if not (System.String.IsNullOrEmpty displayName) then
    48→                                            let parts = displayName.Split(' ', 2)
    49→                                            if parts.Length = 2 && not (parts[1].StartsWith("/")) then
    50→                                                let httpMethod = parts[0].ToLowerInvariant()
    51→                                                let resourceName = parts[1].Replace(" ", "")
    52→                                                operation.OperationId <- httpMethod + resourceName
    53→                                Task.CompletedTask) |> ignore
    54→                        ) |> ignore)
    55→                    .Configure(fun app ->
    56→                        app
    57→                            .UseRouting()
    58→                            .UseEndpoints(fun endpoints ->
    59→                                endpoints.DataSources.Add(TestEndpointDataSource(allEndpoints))
    60→                                endpoints.MapOpenApi() |> ignore
    61→                                endpoints.MapScalarApiReference() |> ignore)
    62→                        |> ignore)
    63→                |> ignore)
    64→
    65→    let host = builder.Build()
    66→    host.Start()
    67→    host.GetTestClient()
    68→
    69→/// Creates a test server WITHOUT OpenAPI enabled
    70→let createPlainTestServer (resources: Resource list) =
    71→    let allEndpoints = resources |> List.collect (fun r -> r.Endpoints |> Array.toList) |> List.toArray
    72→    let builder =
    73→        Host.CreateDefaultBuilder([||])
    74→            .ConfigureWebHost(fun webBuilder ->
    75→                webBuilder
    76→                    .UseTestServer()
    77→                    .ConfigureServices(fun services ->
    78→                        services.AddRouting() |> ignore)
    79→                    .Configure(fun app ->
    80→                        app
    81→                            .UseRouting()
    82→                            .UseEndpoints(fun endpoints ->
    83→                                endpoints.DataSources.Add(TestEndpointDataSource(allEndpoints)))
    84→                        |> ignore)
    85→                |> ignore)
    86→
    87→    let host = builder.Build()
    88→    host.Start()
    89→    host.GetTestClient()
    90→
    91→let simpleHandler : RequestDelegate =
    92→    RequestDelegate(fun ctx -> ctx.Response.WriteAsync("OK"))
    93→
    94→/// Helper to check if a JSON element has a property
    95→let hasProperty (name: string) (element: JsonElement) =
    96→    let mutable value = Unchecked.defaultof<JsonElement>
    97→    element.TryGetProperty(name, &value)
    98→
    99→/// Helper to get and parse the OpenAPI document
   100→let getOpenApiDoc (client: HttpClient) =
   101→    task {
   102→        let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = client.GetAsync("/openapi/v1.json")
   103→        let! (body: string) = response.Content.ReadAsStringAsync()
   104→        return response, JsonDocument.Parse(body : string)
   105→    }
   106→
   107→// ===== US1: Serve OpenAPI Document =====
   108→
   109→[<Tests>]
   110→let us1Tests =
   111→    testList "US1 - Serve OpenAPI Document" [
   112→        testTask "GET /openapi/v1.json returns 200 with valid OpenAPI document" {
   113→            let products =
   114→                resource "/products" {
   115→                    name "Products"
   116→                    get simpleHandler
   117→                }
   118→            let client = createOpenApiTestServer [ products ]
   119→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = client.GetAsync("/openapi/v1.json")
   120→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Should return 200"
   121→
   122→            let! (body: string) = response.Content.ReadAsStringAsync()
   123→            let doc = JsonDocument.Parse(body : string)
   124→            let root = doc.RootElement
   125→
   126→            // Verify it's a valid OpenAPI document
   127→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "openapi" root) "Should have openapi version field"
   128→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "paths" root) "Should have paths field"
   129→
   130→            // Verify the /products path exists
   131→            let paths = root.GetProperty("paths")
   132→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "/products" paths) "Should contain /products path"
   133→
   134→            // Verify GET method exists under /products
   135→            let productsPath = paths.GetProperty("/products")
   136→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "get" productsPath) "Should have GET operation for /products"
   137→        }
   138→
   139→        testTask "OpenAPI document contains auto-derived operationId from resource name" {
   140→            let products =
   141→                resource "/products" {
   142→                    name "Products"
   143→                    get simpleHandler
   144→                }
   145→            let client = createOpenApiTestServer [ products ]
   146→            let! result = getOpenApiDoc client
   147→            let (response: HttpResponseMessage), (doc: JsonDocument) = result
   148→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Should return 200"
   149→            let getOp = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("paths").GetProperty("/products").GetProperty("get")
   150→
   151→            // R8: operationId should be auto-derived from DisplayName "GET Products" -> "getProducts"
   152→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "operationId" getOp) "Should have operationId"
   153→            Expect.equal (getOp.GetProperty("operationId").GetString()) "getProducts" "operationId should be auto-derived from resource name"
   154→        }
   155→
   156→        testTask "OpenAPI document includes multiple endpoints from multiple resources" {
   157→            let products =
   158→                resource "/products" {
   159→                    name "Products"
   160→                    get simpleHandler
   161→                    post simpleHandler
   162→                }
   163→            let users =
   164→                resource "/users" {
   165→                    name "Users"
   166→                    get simpleHandler
   167→                }
   168→            let client = createOpenApiTestServer [ products; users ]
   169→            let! result = getOpenApiDoc client
   170→            let (_, (doc: JsonDocument)) = result
   171→            let paths = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("paths")
   172→
   173→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "/products" paths) "Should contain /products"
   174→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "/users" paths) "Should contain /users"
   175→
   176→            let productsPath = paths.GetProperty("/products")
   177→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "get" productsPath) "Should have GET /products"
   178→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "post" productsPath) "Should have POST /products"
   179→
   180→            let usersPath = paths.GetProperty("/users")
   181→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "get" usersPath) "Should have GET /users"
   182→        }
   183→
   184→        testTask "App without OpenAPI does not expose /openapi/v1.json" {
   185→            let products =
   186→                resource "/products" {
   187→                    name "Products"
   188→                    get simpleHandler
   189→                }
   190→            let client = createPlainTestServer [ products ]
   191→            let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = client.GetAsync("/openapi/v1.json")
   192→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.NotFound "Should return 404 when OpenAPI is not configured"
   193→        }
   194→    ]

   196→// ===== US3: End-to-End Tests with HandlerDefinitions =====
   197→
   198→type Product = { Name: string; Price: decimal }
   199→type CreateProductRequest = { Name: string; Price: decimal }
   200→
   201→[<Tests>]
   202→let us3EndToEndTests =
   203→    testList "US3 - HandlerDefinitions End-to-End" [
   204→        testTask "HandlerDefinition with name and tags appears in OpenAPI document" {
   205→            let createProductHandler =
   206→                handler {
   207→                    name "createProduct"
   208→                    summary "Create a new product"
   209→                    description "Creates a new product in the catalog"
   210→                    tags [ "Products"; "Admin" ]
   211→                    produces typeof<Product> 201
   212→                    accepts typeof<CreateProductRequest>
   213→                    handle (fun (ctx: HttpContext) -> Task.CompletedTask)
   214→                }
   215→
   216→            let products =
   217→                resource "/products" {
   218→                    name "Products"
   219→                    post createProductHandler
   220→                }
   221→
   222→            let client = createOpenApiTestServer [ products ]
   223→            let! result = getOpenApiDoc client
   224→            let (response: HttpResponseMessage), (doc: JsonDocument) = result
   225→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Should return 200"
   226→
   227→            let postOp = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("paths").GetProperty("/products").GetProperty("post")
   228→
   229→            // Check operationId from HandlerDefinition name
   230→            Expect.equal (postOp.GetProperty("operationId").GetString()) "createProduct" "operationId should match handler name"
   231→
   232→            // Check summary and description
   233→            Expect.equal (postOp.GetProperty("summary").GetString()) "Create a new product" "Summary should match"
   234→            Expect.equal (postOp.GetProperty("description").GetString()) "Creates a new product in the catalog" "Description should match"
   235→
   236→            // Check tags
   237→            let tags = postOp.GetProperty("tags")
   238→            Expect.equal (tags.GetArrayLength()) 2 "Should have 2 tags"
   239→            let tagsList = [ for i in 0 .. tags.GetArrayLength() - 1 -> tags[i].GetString() ]
   240→            Expect.containsAll tagsList [ "Products"; "Admin" ] "Tags should match"
   241→        }
   242→
   243→        testTask "HandlerDefinition produces metadata appears as response in OpenAPI" {
   244→            let getProductHandler =
   245→                handler {
   246→                    name "getProduct"
   247→                    produces typeof<Product> 200
   248→                    produces typeof<Product> 201
   249→                    producesEmpty 404
   250→                    handle (fun (ctx: HttpContext) -> Task.CompletedTask)
   251→                }
   252→
   253→            let products =
   254→                resource "/products/{id}" {
   255→                    get getProductHandler
   256→                }
   257→
   258→            let client = createOpenApiTestServer [ products ]
   259→            let! result = getOpenApiDoc client
   260→            let (response: HttpResponseMessage), (doc: JsonDocument) = result
   261→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Should return 200"
   262→
   263→            let getOp = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("paths").GetProperty("/products/{id}").GetProperty("get")
   264→            let responses = getOp.GetProperty("responses")
   265→
   266→            // Check 200 response with Product schema
   267→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "200" responses) "Should have 200 response"
   268→            let resp200 = responses.GetProperty("200")
   269→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "content" resp200) "200 should have content"
   270→
   271→            // Check 201 response
   272→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "201" responses) "Should have 201 response"
   273→
   274→            // Check 404 empty response
   275→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "404" responses) "Should have 404 response"
   276→            let resp404 = responses.GetProperty("404")
   277→            // Empty responses typically don't have content or have empty content
   278→            let has404Content = hasProperty "content" resp404
   279→            if has404Content then
   280→                let content404 = resp404.GetProperty("content")
   281→                Expect.equal (content404.EnumerateObject() |> Seq.length) 0 "404 should have no content types"
   282→        }
   283→
   284→        testTask "HandlerDefinition accepts metadata appears as requestBody in OpenAPI" {
   285→            let createHandler =
   286→                handler {
   287→                    name "createProduct"
   288→                    accepts typeof<CreateProductRequest>
   289→                    produces typeof<Product> 201
   290→                    handle (fun (ctx: HttpContext) -> Task.CompletedTask)
   291→                }
   292→
   293→            let products =
   294→                resource "/products" {
   295→                    post createHandler
   296→                }
   297→
   298→            let client = createOpenApiTestServer [ products ]
   299→            let! result = getOpenApiDoc client
   300→            let (response: HttpResponseMessage), (doc: JsonDocument) = result
   301→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Should return 200"
   302→
   303→            let postOp = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("paths").GetProperty("/products").GetProperty("post")
   304→
   305→            // Check requestBody
   306→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "requestBody" postOp) "Should have requestBody"
   307→            let requestBody = postOp.GetProperty("requestBody")
   308→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "content" requestBody) "RequestBody should have content"
   309→            let content = requestBody.GetProperty("content")
   310→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "application/json" content) "Should accept application/json"
   311→        }
   312→
   313→        testTask "Resource with mixed plain handlers and HandlerDefinitions" {
   314→            let plainGetHandler : RequestDelegate = RequestDelegate(fun ctx -> ctx.Response.WriteAsync("OK"))
   315→
   316→            let createHandler =
   317→                handler {
   318→                    name "createProduct"
   319→                    summary "Create product"
   320→                    produces typeof<Product> 201
   321→                    handle (fun (ctx: HttpContext) -> Task.CompletedTask)
   322→                }
   323→
   324→            let products =
   325→                resource "/products" {
   326→                    name "Products"
   327→                    get plainGetHandler
   328→                    post createHandler
   329→                }
   330→
   331→            let client = createOpenApiTestServer [ products ]
   332→            let! result = getOpenApiDoc client
   333→            let (response: HttpResponseMessage), (doc: JsonDocument) = result
   334→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Should return 200"
   335→
   336→            let productsPath = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("paths").GetProperty("/products")
   337→
   338→            // Plain handler should use auto-derived operationId
   339→            let getOp = productsPath.GetProperty("get")
   340→            Expect.equal (getOp.GetProperty("operationId").GetString()) "getProducts" "GET should have auto-derived operationId"
   341→            Expect.isFalse (hasProperty "summary" getOp) "GET should not have summary"
   342→
   343→            // HandlerDefinition should have its metadata
   344→            let postOp = productsPath.GetProperty("post")
   345→            Expect.equal (postOp.GetProperty("operationId").GetString()) "createProduct" "POST should have handler-defined operationId"
   346→            Expect.equal (postOp.GetProperty("summary").GetString()) "Create product" "POST should have summary"
   347→        }
   348→
   349→        testTask "HandlerDefinition with custom content types for content negotiation" {
   350→            let getHandler =
   351→                handler {
   352→                    name "getProduct"
   353→                    produces typeof<Product> 200 ["application/xml"; "application/json"]
   354→                    handle (fun (ctx: HttpContext) -> Task.CompletedTask)
   355→                }
   356→
   357→            let createHandler =
   358→                handler {
   359→                    name "createProduct"
   360→                    accepts typeof<CreateProductRequest> ["application/xml"]
   361→                    produces typeof<Product> 201
   362→                    handle (fun (ctx: HttpContext) -> Task.CompletedTask)
   363→                }
   364→
   365→            let products =
   366→                resource "/products" {
   367→                    get getHandler
   368→                    post createHandler
   369→                }
   370→
   371→            let client = createOpenApiTestServer [ products ]
   372→            let! result = getOpenApiDoc client
   373→            let (response: HttpResponseMessage), (doc: JsonDocument) = result
   374→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Should return 200"
   375→
   376→            let productsPath = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("paths").GetProperty("/products")
   377→
   378→            // Check GET response supports both XML and JSON
   379→            let getOp = productsPath.GetProperty("get")
   380→            let getResp200 = getOp.GetProperty("responses").GetProperty("200")
   381→            let getContent = getResp200.GetProperty("content")
   382→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "application/xml" getContent) "GET 200 should support XML"
   383→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "application/json" getContent) "GET 200 should support JSON"
   384→
   385→            // Check POST requestBody accepts XML
   386→            let postOp = productsPath.GetProperty("post")
   387→            let postRequestBody = postOp.GetProperty("requestBody")
   388→            let postContent = postRequestBody.GetProperty("content")
   389→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "application/xml" postContent) "POST should accept XML"
   390→        }
   391→    ]

1→module Frank.OpenApi.Tests.SchemaTests
     2→
     3→open System
     4→open System.Net
     5→open System.Net.Http
     6→open System.Text.Json
     7→open System.Threading.Tasks
     8→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder
     9→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http
    10→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.Metadata
    11→open Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing
    12→open Expecto
    13→open Frank.Builder
    14→
    15→// ===== Sample F# Types for Schema Testing =====
    16→
    17→type Product =
    18→    { Name: string
    19→      Price: decimal
    20→      Description: string option
    21→      InStock: bool }
    22→
    23→type Shape =
    24→    | Circle of radius: float
    25→    | Rectangle of width: float * height: float
    26→    | Point
    27→
    28→type OrderWithCollections =
    29→    { Items: string list
    30→      Tags: Set<string>
    31→      Attributes: Map<string, string> }
    32→
    33→// ===== Test Infrastructure =====
    34→
    35→let simpleHandler : RequestDelegate =
    36→    RequestDelegate(fun ctx -> ctx.Response.WriteAsync("OK"))
    37→
    38→/// Helper to check if a JSON element has a property
    39→let hasProperty (name: string) (element: JsonElement) =
    40→    let mutable value = Unchecked.defaultof<JsonElement>
    41→    element.TryGetProperty(name, &value)
    42→
    43→/// Helper to get and parse the OpenAPI document
    44→let getOpenApiDoc (client: HttpClient) =
    45→    task {
    46→        let! (response: HttpResponseMessage) = client.GetAsync("/openapi/v1.json")
    47→        let! (body: string) = response.Content.ReadAsStringAsync()
    48→        return response, JsonDocument.Parse(body : string)
    49→    }
    50→
    51→/// Rebuild endpoints from a resource, adding extra metadata
    52→let addMetadataToEndpoints (extraMeta: obj list) (r: Resource) : Resource =
    53→    let endpoints =
    54→        r.Endpoints
    55→        |> Array.map (fun e ->
    56→            let re = e :?> RouteEndpoint
    57→            let builder = RouteEndpointBuilder(re.RequestDelegate, re.RoutePattern, re.Order)
    58→            builder.DisplayName <- e.DisplayName
    59→            for m in e.Metadata do
    60→                builder.Metadata.Add(m)
    61→            for m in extraMeta do
    62→                builder.Metadata.Add(m)
    63→            builder.Build())
    64→    { Endpoints = endpoints }
    65→
    66→/// Find a schema with properties, following $ref into components if needed
    67→let findSchemaWithProps (doc: JsonDocument) (schema: JsonElement) (matchProps: string list) =
    68→    if hasProperty "properties" schema then
    69→        Some schema
    70→    elif hasProperty "$ref" schema then
    71→        if hasProperty "components" doc.RootElement then
    72→            let components = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("components").GetProperty("schemas")
    73→            let mutable result = None
    74→            for prop in components.EnumerateObject() do
    75→                let s = prop.Value
    76→                if hasProperty "properties" s then
    77→                    let p = s.GetProperty("properties")
    78→                    if matchProps |> List.forall (fun name -> hasProperty name p) then
    79→                        result <- Some s
    80→            result
    81→        else None
    82→    else None
    83→
    84→// ===== US2: Document Request/Response Types for F# Types =====
    85→
    86→[<Tests>]
    87→let us2Tests =
    88→    testList "US2 - F# Type Schemas" [
    89→        testTask "Record type produces correct schema with properties and required fields" {
    90→            let testResource =
    91→                resource "/products" { name "Products"; get simpleHandler }
    92→                |> addMetadataToEndpoints [ ProducesResponseTypeMetadata(200, typeof<Product>, [| "application/json" |]) ]
    93→
    94→            let client = OpenApiDocumentTests.createOpenApiTestServer [ testResource ]
    95→            let! result = getOpenApiDoc client
    96→            let (response: HttpResponseMessage), (doc: JsonDocument) = result
    97→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Should return 200"
    98→
    99→            let getOp = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("paths").GetProperty("/products").GetProperty("get")
   100→            let responses = getOp.GetProperty("responses")
   101→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "200" responses) "Should have 200 response"
   102→
   103→            let resp200 = responses.GetProperty("200")
   104→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "content" resp200) "200 response should have content"
   105→            let jsonContent = resp200.GetProperty("content").GetProperty("application/json")
   106→            Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "schema" jsonContent) "Should have schema"
   107→
   108→            let schema = jsonContent.GetProperty("schema")
   109→            match findSchemaWithProps doc schema [ "name"; "price" ] with
   110→            | Some s ->
   111→                let props = s.GetProperty("properties")
   112→                Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "name" props) "Should have name property"
   113→                Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "price" props) "Should have price property"
   114→                Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "description" props) "Should have description property"
   115→                Expect.isTrue (hasProperty "inStock" props) "Should have inStock property"
   116→            | None ->
   117→                failtest "Schema should have properties (inline or via $ref)"
   118→        }
   119→
   120→        testTask "Option type fields are nullable in schema" {
   121→            let testResource =
   122→                resource "/items" { name "Items"; get simpleHandler }
   123→                |> addMetadataToEndpoints [ ProducesResponseTypeMetadata(200, typeof<Product>, [| "application/json" |]) ]
   124→
   125→            let client = OpenApiDocumentTests.createOpenApiTestServer [ testResource ]
   126→            let! result = getOpenApiDoc client
   127→            let (response: HttpResponseMessage), (doc: JsonDocument) = result
   128→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Should return 200"
   129→
   130→            let getOp = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("paths").GetProperty("/items").GetProperty("get")
   131→            let resp200 = getOp.GetProperty("responses").GetProperty("200")
   132→            let jsonContent = resp200.GetProperty("content").GetProperty("application/json")
   133→            let schema = jsonContent.GetProperty("schema")
   134→
   135→            match findSchemaWithProps doc schema [ "name"; "description" ] with
   136→            | Some s ->
   137→                let props = s.GetProperty("properties")
   138→                let descProp = props.GetProperty("description")
   139→                // Option types should be nullable — check for "nullable: true" or anyOf/oneOf with null type
   140→                // On .NET 10, nullable is represented in the type field using JsonSchemaType flags
   141→                let isNullable =
   142→                    (hasProperty "nullable" descProp && descProp.GetProperty("nullable").GetBoolean())
   143→                    || (hasProperty "anyOf" descProp)
   144→                    || (hasProperty "oneOf" descProp)
   145→                    || (hasProperty "type" descProp && descProp.GetProperty("type").ToString().Contains("null"))
   146→                Expect.isTrue isNullable "Option field 'description' should be nullable"
   147→
   148→                // Required fields should NOT include the option field
   149→                if hasProperty "required" s then
   150→                    let required = s.GetProperty("required")
   151→                    let requiredFields = [
   152→                        for i in 0 .. required.GetArrayLength() - 1 ->
   153→                            required[i].GetString()
   154→                    ]
   155→                    Expect.isFalse (requiredFields |> List.contains "description") "Option field should not be in required"
   156→                    Expect.isTrue (requiredFields |> List.contains "name") "Non-option field 'name' should be required"
   157→            | None ->
   158→                failtest "Could not find schema with properties"
   159→        }
   160→
   161→        testTask "Collection types produce array schemas" {
   162→            let testResource =
   163→                resource "/orders" { name "Orders"; get simpleHandler }
   164→                |> addMetadataToEndpoints [ ProducesResponseTypeMetadata(200, typeof<OrderWithCollections>, [| "application/json" |]) ]
   165→
   166→            let client = OpenApiDocumentTests.createOpenApiTestServer [ testResource ]
   167→            let! result = getOpenApiDoc client
   168→            let (response: HttpResponseMessage), (doc: JsonDocument) = result
   169→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Should return 200"
   170→
   171→            let getOp = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("paths").GetProperty("/orders").GetProperty("get")
   172→            let resp200 = getOp.GetProperty("responses").GetProperty("200")
   173→            let jsonContent = resp200.GetProperty("content").GetProperty("application/json")
   174→            let schema = jsonContent.GetProperty("schema")
   175→
   176→            match findSchemaWithProps doc schema [ "items"; "tags" ] with
   177→            | Some s ->
   178→                let props = s.GetProperty("properties")
   179→                // list<string> should be an array
   180→                let itemsProp = props.GetProperty("items")
   181→                Expect.equal (itemsProp.GetProperty("type").GetString()) "array" "List should be array type"
   182→
   183→                // Set<string> should also be an array
   184→                let tagsProp = props.GetProperty("tags")
   185→                Expect.equal (tagsProp.GetProperty("type").GetString()) "array" "Set should be array type"
   186→
   187→                // Map<string,string> should be object with additionalProperties
   188→                let attrProp = props.GetProperty("attributes")
   189→                let isMapType =
   190→                    (hasProperty "type" attrProp && attrProp.GetProperty("type").GetString() = "object")
   191→                    || hasProperty "additionalProperties" attrProp
   192→                Expect.isTrue isMapType "Map should be object with additionalProperties"
   193→            | None ->
   194→                failtest "Could not find schema with properties"
   195→        }
   196→
   197→        testTask "Discriminated union produces anyOf or oneOf schema" {
   198→            let testResource =
   199→                resource "/shapes" { name "Shapes"; get simpleHandler }
   200→                |> addMetadataToEndpoints [ ProducesResponseTypeMetadata(200, typeof<Shape>, [| "application/json" |]) ]
   201→
   202→            let client = OpenApiDocumentTests.createOpenApiTestServer [ testResource ]
   203→            let! result = getOpenApiDoc client
   204→            let (response: HttpResponseMessage), (doc: JsonDocument) = result
   205→            Expect.equal response.StatusCode HttpStatusCode.OK "Should return 200"
   206→
   207→            let getOp = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("paths").GetProperty("/shapes").GetProperty("get")
   208→            let resp200 = getOp.GetProperty("responses").GetProperty("200")
   209→            let jsonContent = resp200.GetProperty("content").GetProperty("application/json")
   210→            let schema = jsonContent.GetProperty("schema")
   211→
   212→            // DU should produce anyOf/oneOf either inline or via $ref
   213→            let hasUnionSchema =
   214→                hasProperty "anyOf" schema
   215→                || hasProperty "oneOf" schema
   216→                || hasProperty "$ref" schema
   217→            Expect.isTrue hasUnionSchema "DU type should produce anyOf, oneOf, or $ref schema"
   218→
   219→            // If it's a $ref, check components for the union schema
   220→            if hasProperty "$ref" schema then
   221→                if hasProperty "components" doc.RootElement then
   222→                    let components = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("components").GetProperty("schemas")
   223→                    let mutable foundUnion = false
   224→                    for prop in components.EnumerateObject() do
   225→                        let s = prop.Value
   226→                        if hasProperty "anyOf" s || hasProperty "oneOf" s then
   227→                            foundUnion <- true
   228→                    Expect.isTrue foundUnion "Should have union schema in components with anyOf/oneOf"
   229→        }
   230→    ]
   231→

1→module Program
     2→

     6→let main args =
     7→    runTestsInAssemblyWithCLIArgs [] args

1→namespace Frank.Cli.Core.Commands
     2→
     3→open System
     4→open System.IO
     5→open System.Text.Json
     6→open VDS.RDF
     7→open VDS.RDF.Parsing
     8→open VDS.RDF.Writing
     9→open Frank.Cli.Core.State
    10→
    11→/// Generates final OWL/XML and SHACL artifacts from extraction state.
    12→module CompileCommand =
    13→
    14→    type CompileResult =
    15→        { OntologyPath: string
    16→          ShapesPath: string
    17→          ManifestPath: string
    18→          EmbeddedResourceNames: string list }
    19→
    20→    let private backupState (statePath: string) =
    21→        if File.Exists statePath then
    22→            let dir = Path.GetDirectoryName statePath
    23→            let backupDir = Path.Combine(dir, "backups")
    24→            if not (Directory.Exists backupDir) then
    25→                Directory.CreateDirectory backupDir |> ignore
    26→            let timestamp = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.ToString("yyyyMMddTHHmmssZ")
    27→            let backupPath = Path.Combine(backupDir, $"extraction-state-%s{timestamp}.json")
    28→            File.Copy(statePath, backupPath)
    29→
    30→    let private verifyRoundTrip (ontologyPath: string) (shapesPath: string) (manifestPath: string) =
    31→        // Re-parse ontology
    32→        let ontologyGraph = new Graph()
    33→        let rdfXmlParser = RdfXmlParser()
    34→        use ontologyReader = new StreamReader(ontologyPath)
    35→        rdfXmlParser.Load(ontologyGraph, ontologyReader)
    36→
    37→        // Re-parse shapes
    38→        let shapesGraph = new Graph()
    39→        let turtleParser = TurtleParser()
    40→        use shapesReader = new StreamReader(shapesPath)
    41→        turtleParser.Load(shapesGraph, shapesReader)
    42→
    43→        // Re-parse manifest
    44→        let manifestJson = File.ReadAllText(manifestPath)
    45→        JsonDocument.Parse(manifestJson) |> ignore
    46→
    47→    let execute (projectPath: string) (outputDir: string option) : Result<CompileResult, string> =
    48→        let projectDir = Path.GetDirectoryName projectPath
    49→        let statePath = ExtractionState.defaultStatePath projectDir
    50→
    51→        match ExtractionState.load statePath with
    52→        | Error _ -> Error "No extraction state found. Run 'frank-cli extract' first."
    53→        | Ok state ->
    54→
    55→        try
    56→            let outDir =
    57→                outputDir
    58→                |> Option.defaultValue (Path.Combine(projectDir, "obj", "frank-cli"))
    59→
    60→            if not (Directory.Exists outDir) then
    61→                Directory.CreateDirectory outDir |> ignore
    62→
    63→            // Back up existing state before overwriting
    64→            backupState statePath
    65→
    66→            // Write ontology.owl.xml
    67→            let ontologyPath = Path.Combine(outDir, "ontology.owl.xml")
    68→            do
    69→                let rdfXmlWriter = RdfXmlWriter()
    70→                use ontologyStream = File.Create ontologyPath
    71→                use ontologySw = new StreamWriter(ontologyStream)
    72→                rdfXmlWriter.Save(state.Ontology, ontologySw :> TextWriter)
    73→
    74→            // Write shapes.shacl.ttl
    75→            let shapesPath = Path.Combine(outDir, "shapes.shacl.ttl")
    76→            do
    77→                let turtleWriter = CompressingTurtleWriter()
    78→                use shapesStream = File.Create shapesPath
    79→                use shapesSw = new StreamWriter(shapesStream)
    80→                turtleWriter.Save(state.Shapes, shapesSw :> TextWriter)
    81→
    82→            // Write manifest.json
    83→            let manifestPath = Path.Combine(outDir, "manifest.json")
    84→            do
    85→                use manifestStream = File.Create manifestPath
    86→
    87→                use writer =
    88→                    new Utf8JsonWriter(manifestStream, JsonWriterOptions(Indented = true))
    89→
    90→                writer.WriteStartObject()
    91→                writer.WriteString("version", state.Metadata.ToolVersion)
    92→                writer.WriteString("baseUri", state.Metadata.BaseUri.AbsoluteUri)
    93→                writer.WriteString("sourceHash", state.Metadata.SourceHash)
    94→
    95→                writer.WriteStartArray("vocabularies")
    96→
    97→                for v in state.Metadata.Vocabularies do
    98→                    writer.WriteStringValue v
    99→
   100→                writer.WriteEndArray()
   101→
   102→                writer.WriteString("generatedAt", DateTimeOffset.UtcNow)
   103→                writer.WriteEndObject()
   104→                writer.Flush()
   105→
   106→            // Round-trip verification: re-parse each file to confirm validity
   107→            verifyRoundTrip ontologyPath shapesPath manifestPath
   108→
   109→            Ok
   110→                { OntologyPath = ontologyPath
   111→                  ShapesPath = shapesPath
   112→                  ManifestPath = manifestPath
   113→                  EmbeddedResourceNames =
   114→                    [ "Frank.Semantic.ontology.owl.xml"
   115→                      "Frank.Semantic.shapes.shacl.ttl"
   116→                      "Frank.Semantic.manifest.json" ] }
   117→        with ex ->
   118→            Error $"Compile failed: {ex.Message}"
   119→

1→namespace Frank.Cli.Core.Commands
     2→
     3→open System.IO
     4→open Frank.Cli.Core.State
     5→
     6→/// Structured diff between extraction states.
     7→module DiffCommand =
     8→
     9→    type DiffCommandResult =
    10→        { Diff: DiffResult
    11→          FormattedDiff: string }
    12→
    13→    let private findLatestBackup (projectDir: string) : string option =
    14→        let backupDir = Path.Combine(projectDir, "obj", "frank-cli", "backups")
    15→        if Directory.Exists backupDir then
    16→            Directory.GetFiles(backupDir, "extraction-state-*.json")
    17→            |> Array.sortDescending
    18→            |> Array.tryHead
    19→        else
    20→            None
    21→
    22→    let private emptyResult () =
    23→        let emptyDiff =
    24→            { Added = []; Removed = []; Modified = [] }
    25→        Ok
    26→            { Diff = emptyDiff
    27→              FormattedDiff = "No previous state found. Nothing to compare." }
    28→
    29→    let execute (projectPath: string) (previousPath: string option) : Result<DiffCommandResult, string> =
    30→        let projectDir = Path.GetDirectoryName projectPath
    31→        let currentStatePath = ExtractionState.defaultStatePath projectDir
    32→
    33→        match ExtractionState.load currentStatePath with
    34→        | Error e -> Error $"Failed to load current state: {e}"
    35→        | Ok currentState ->
    36→
    37→        let resolvedPrevious =
    38→            match previousPath with
    39→            | Some p -> Some p
    40→            | None -> findLatestBackup projectDir
    41→
    42→        match resolvedPrevious with
    43→        | None -> emptyResult ()
    44→        | Some prevPath ->
    45→
    46→        match ExtractionState.load prevPath with
    47→        | Error e -> Error $"Failed to load previous state: {e}"
    48→        | Ok previousState ->
    49→
    50→        let diff = DiffEngine.diffStates previousState currentState
    51→        let formatted = DiffEngine.formatDiff diff
    52→
    53→        Ok
    54→            { Diff = diff
    55→              FormattedDiff = formatted }
    56→
